Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN1}House Arryn of the Eyrie
Faction.text: House Arryn of the Eyrie is the ruling Great House of the Vale of Arryn and holds dominion over the Vale’s noble houses. Their seat is the Eyrie, a mountain stronghold located in the Mountains of the Moon. House Arryn traces its lineage to the Andal warlord Ser Artys Arryn, who led the Andal conquest of the Vale. Their sigil is a white falcon and crescent moon on a sky-blue field, and their words are 'As High as Honor.'

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN2}House Royce of Runestone
Faction.text: House Royce of Runestone is one of the principal noble houses of the Vale of Arryn and is sworn to House Arryn of the Eyrie. Their seat is Runestone, located on the eastern coast of the Vale. House Royce traces its lineage to the First Men and is among the oldest houses in the Vale. Members of the house are known to wear and preserve ancient bronze armor engraved with runes, a tradition associated with their First Men heritage. Their heraldry displays black iron studs on a field of bronze, bordered in black, and their words are 'We Remember.'

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN3}House Redfort of the Redfort
Faction.text: House Redfort of Redfort is a principal noble house of the Vale of Arryn, sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Redfort, and their arms are described as a red castle on a white field within a red embattled border.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN4}House Waynwood of Ironoaks
Faction.text: House Waynwood of Ironoaks is a noble house of the Vale of Arryn, sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Ironoaks, located in the Vale. House Waynwood is counted among the principal houses of the region. Their heraldry depicts a black broken wheel on a green field, and their words are 'Ever Constant.'

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN5}House Hunter of Longbow Hall
Faction.text: House Hunter of Longbow Hall is a noble house of the Vale of Arryn, sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Longbow Hall, located in the Vale. House Hunter is counted among the landed noble houses of the region. Their heraldry depicts five silver arrows, points downward, on a brown field.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN6}House Sunderland of the Three Sisters
Faction.text: House Sunderland of the Three Sisters is a noble house sworn to House Arryn of the Vale of Arryn. They rule the Three Sisters, an island group located in the Bite between the Vale and the North. Their seat is Sisterton, the principal settlement of the islands. House Sunderland is historically associated with piracy and smuggling in the waters of the Bite. Their heraldry depicts three silver mermaids on a field of blue and green waves.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN7}House Corbray of Heartshome
Faction.text: House Corbray of Heart's Home is a noble house from Heart's Home in the Vale of Arryn. The ancestral Valyrian steel longsword of the Corbrays is Lady Forlorn. They blazon their arms with three black ravens in flight holding three red hearts, on white. The Corbrays were an Andal house during the coming of the Andals to Westeros. Corwyn Corbray conquered the Fingers by defeating Houses Brightstone and Shell, claiming for himself the title of Lord of the Five Fingers. When the First Men began to unite against the Andals, King Robar II Royce slew Qyle Corbray, King of the Fingers. The Corbrays claim that Ser Jaime Corbray killed Robar in the Battle of the Seven Stars.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN8}House Grafton of Gulltown
Faction.text: House Grafton of Gulltown is a noble house from the Vale of Arryn, one of the main families sworn to House Arryn of the Eyrie. They rule over Gulltown, a main port and populated city, as well as the only city in the Vale. The Graftons were founded during the coming of the Andals to the Vale. Ser Gerold Grafton was an Andal knight from Andalos who supported King Osgood III Shett of Gulltown in battle against House Royce, marrying his daughters to Shett and his heir, while taking King Osgood's daughter to wife. While the Shetts defeated the Royces, Osgood III did not survive the battle, with some claiming that it was Ser Gerold who struck him down. When King Robar II Royce united the First Men against the Andals, Royce sent his sister instead Gulltown to persuade the Shetts to rise against the Graftons and open the city gate, allowing Royce to take the city by storm. However, Royce was eventually defeated and rule of the Vale fell to House Arryn.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN9}House Belmore of Strongsong
Faction.text: House Belmore of Strongsong is a noble house of the Vale of Arryn, sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Strongsong, located in the Vale. House Belmore is counted among the principal noble houses of the region. Their heraldry displays a field of purple with white bells.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN10}House Lynderly of Snakewood
Faction.text: House Lynderly is a noble house of the Vale of Arryn sworn to House Arryn of the Eyrie. Their seat is Snakewood, located in the Vale. The head of the house bears the title Lord of Snakewood.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN11}House Templeton of Ninestars
Faction.text: House Templeton is a noble house of the Vale sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Ninestars, and the head of the house is styled the Knight of Ninestars. During the Andal invasion of the Vale, Ser Luceon Templeton, the Knight of Ninestars, was an Andal knight that participated in the Battle of the Seven Stars under the leadership of Ser Artys Arryn. Ser Luceon may have slain King Robar II Royce during the battle.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN12}House Hardyng
Faction.text: House Hardyng is a house of landed knights in the Vale of Arryn. The Hardyngs blazon their arms with a field of red and white diamonds.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN13}House Waxley of Wickenden
Faction.text: House Waxley is a noble house of the Vale sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Wickenden, a port town on the eastern coast of the Vale. Like many Vale houses, the Waxleys are of Andal descent, tracing their origins to the Andal migration that established the Faith of the Seven and Andal rule in the region. It is famous for making scented beeswax candles.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN14}House Upcliff of Witch Isle
Faction.text: House Upcliff is a noble house of the Vale sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Witch Isle, an island in the Bite off the eastern coast of the Vale. The Upcliffs are counted among the ancient houses of the Vale and are noted for maintaining certain old traditions associated with the island.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN15}House Shett of Gull Tower
Faction.text: House Shett of Gull Tower is a noble house of the Vale sworn to House Arryn. Their seat is Gull Tower near Gulltown. House Shett is an Andal house that came to prominence in the Vale following the Andal invasions. A branch of House Shett once ruled Gulltown before being supplanted by House Grafton.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN16}House Melcolm of Old Anchor
Faction.text: House Melcolm of Old Anchor is a noble house from Old Anchor in the Vale of Arryn. It is one of the principal houses sworn to House Arryn. They blazon their arms with a rusty anchor on turquoise beneath yellow chief with three blue plates.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN17}House Coldwater of Coldwater Burn
Faction.text: House Coldwater of Coldwater Burn is a noble house from Coldwater Burn in the Vale of Arryn. They are sworn to the Royces of Runestone. The Coldwaters are of First Men origin. During the coming of the Andals to the Vale, they allied with King Robar II Royce against the invading Andals. Robar's forces were defeated in the Battle of the Seven Stars, however, and the Coldwaters submitted to Artys I Arryn, the new King of Mountain and Vale.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN18}House Elesham of the Paps
Faction.text: House Elesham of the Paps is a noble house from the Paps in the Vale. They blazon their arms with a double pile inverted, stone on pink, a black mullet between. House Elesham were once independent rulers of the Paps, until they bent the knee to Hugo the Hopeful, a King of Mountain and Vale of House Arryn.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN19}House Pryor of Pebble
Faction.text: House Pryor of Pebble is a noble house from Pebble in the Vale. They blazon their arms with a partial eclipse, black moon over yellow sun, on dusty pink.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN20}House Tollett of the Grey Glen
Faction.text: House Tollett of the Grey Glen is a house from the Grey Glen in the Vale of Arryn. They blazon their shield as pily grey and black.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN21}House Hersy of Newkeep
Faction.text: House Hersy of Newkeep is a noble house from Newkeep in the Vale. It is one of the principal houses sworn to House Arryn. They blazon their arms with a white winged chalice on pink.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN22}House Longthorpe of Longsister
Faction.text: House Longthorpe of Longsister is a noble house from Longsister in the Vale of Arryn. They are vassals of House Sunderland.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN23}House Torrent of Littlesister
Faction.text: House Torrent of Littlesister is a noble house from Littlesister in the Vale of Arryn. They are vassals of House Sunderland.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN24}House Egen
Faction.text: House Egen is a noble house from the Vale, one of the principal families sworn to House Arryn. They blazon their arms with a yellow sun, white crescent moon, and silver star on blue chief above white. Their motto is 'By Day or Night'.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN25}House Ruthermont
Faction.text: House Ruthermont is a noble house from the Vale of Arryn. They blazon their arms with five black starfish on a gold pale, on pean.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN26}House Moore
Faction.text: House Moore is a noble house of the Vale, sworn to House Arryn of the Eyrie, and they bear three bronze spearheads on white within a bronze embattled border. Their line is counted among those Vale houses that have long upheld the rule of the Arryns, and in ages past Ser Maladon Moore rose to the Kingsguard of King Maegor I Targaryen, though his service ended in execution, a reminder that even those raised high in royal favor are not beyond peril.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN27}House Donniger
Faction.text: House Donniger is a noble house of the Vale, sworn to House Arryn of the Eyrie, and they bear a red sun rising from a grey-green sea against a yellow sky. Their house stands among the lesser lords of the Vale, bound by oath to serve the Arryns and to answer the call of their liege, as is the duty of all who hold land and title beneath the falcon banner.

Faction.name: {=LOC_VALECLAN28}House Arryn of Gulltown
Faction.text: House Arryn of Gulltown is a cadet branch of House Arryn, seated in Gulltown, and they bear the falcon and moon of their kin, oft depicted in gold. Founded during the reign of King Jaehaerys I Targaryen, this branch turned to trade and grew wealthy through commerce, earning a reputation distinct from their highborn cousins of the Eyrie. Isembard Arryn, called the Gilded Falcon, rose as head of the house and pressed his claim to the Eyrie after the death of Lady Jeyne Arryn, yet was defeated by Joffrey Arryn in the Vale’s civil strife, a conflict that drew in the Iron Throne and saw the death of a Hand of the King before his cause was broken. He later served as Master of Coin, reforming taxes and providing relief in the wake of the Lysene Spring, when the collapse of the Rogare Bank brought hardship across the realm.

Faction.name: {=LOC_BLACKFYRE_CLAN}House Blackfyre
Faction.text: House Blackfyre is a noble house from the Crownlands. A cadet branch of House Targaryen, House Blackfyre was founded by Daemon Blackfyre, a legitimized Great Bastard of King Aegon IV Targaryen. House Blackfyre is named for the Valyrian steel sword of the same name. The Blackfyre sigil is a black three-headed dragon, breathing black fire on red, the Targaryen sigil with the colors reversed. The Blackfyres share similar genetic traits to the Targaryens with platinum blond hair, violet eyes and the premonition-like dragon dream ability. Daemon was granted a tract of land along the Blackwater Rush and the right to build a keep there by his half-brother, King Daeron II Targaryen.

Faction.name: {=LOC_TARGARYEN_CLAN}House Targaryen
Faction.text: House Targaryen sits the Iron Throne under King Daeron II, called Daeron the Good. The dynasty still draws its authority from Aegon's Conquest, when Aegon I Targaryen and his sister-wives forged the Kingdoms with dragonfire. The deep scars of the Dance of the Dragons remain part of the royal legacy; that recent civil war shattered the strength of the house and led to the extinction of dragons in the reign of Aegon III Targaryen. Though dragons are gone, the Targaryens continue to rule through lineage, alliances, and the authority of the crown. King Daeron II reigns after the death of his father, Aegon IV Targaryen, whose deathbed legitimization of his bastards now weighs heavily upon the realm. Among them stands Daemon Blackfyre, whose claim divides loyalties across Westeros. Dorne is now part of the Seven Kingdoms through marriage alliance rather than conquest, a union achieved during Daeron’s rule, which many especially near the southern borders detest. At court stand his sons Baelor Breakspear, Aerys, Rhaegel, Maekar and his half-brother Brynden Rivers, called 'Bloodraven' as the realm moves toward open conflict in what will become known as the First Blackfyre Rebellion.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_1}House Velaryon of Driftmark
Faction.text: House Velaryon of Driftmark stands as one of the oldest and most storied Valyrian houses in Westeros, sworn to the Iron Throne and bound to House Targaryen by blood, marriage, and shared heritage. Their power rises during the Targaryen dynasty’s early centuries, most notably under Lord Corlys Velaryon, the Sea Snake, whose legendary voyages brought immense wealth and prestige, and whose descendants play central roles in the succession struggles that culminated in the Dance of the Dragons. Though the civil war devastated their strength and fleet, the Velaryons remain loyal servants of the crown in the generations that followed, continuing to provide ships, admirals, and royal consorts to the Targaryen kings. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Velaryon still holds Driftmark and commands respect as the foremost naval power of the realm, standing firmly aligned with the legitimate royal line as tensions rise toward the First Blackfyre Rebellion. House Velaryon of Driftmark rules the island of Driftmark, the largest island of Blackwater Bay in the Crownlands. Their castles include Driftmark and High Tide. The head of House Velaryon is titled Lord of the Tides and Master of Driftmark. Their arms depict a silver seahorse on sea green. Their words are 'The Old, the True, the Brave'.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_2}House Hayford of Hayford Castle
Faction.text: House Hayford of Hayford is a noble house from Hayford in the crownlands, less than a day's ride from King's Landing. It is sworn directly to the Iron Throne. Their blazon is green fretty over gold, a green pale wavy.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_3}House Rosby of Rosby Castle
Faction.text: House Rosby of Rosby Castle is a noble house from the Crownlands. They rule the castle of Rosby and its village, which are located just northeast of King's Landing. The Rosbys are not known for their robustness. Their blazon consists into three red chevronels on ermine. House Rosby was one of the first houses to yield peacefully to House Targaryen during Aegon's Conquest, surrendering to Rhaenys Targaryen and Meraxes. The Rosby lands became part of the Crownlands surrounding King's Landing. Lord Jon Rosby was named Warden of the Sands by King Aegon I Targaryen during the First Dornish War, but Jon was killed in the Defenestration of Sunspear. Lord Rosby was imprisoned in the Red Keep at the start of the Dance of the Dragons for supporting Rhaenyra Targaryen and the blacks instead of Aegon II Targaryen and the greens. After Ser Criston Cole became Hand of the King to Aegon, Lord Rosby joined the greens to avoid being executed by the King's Justice. Lord Rosby was later executed by Queen Rhaenyra Targaryen during the Fall of King's Landing. Despite the Dance of the Dragons, House Rosby persevered.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_4}House Stokeworth of Stokeworth Castle
Faction.text: House Stokeworth of Stokeworth is a noble house from the Crownlands. Sworn directly to King's Landing, they live at nearby Castle Stokeworth north of the capital. They blazon their arms with a white lamb holding a golden goblet on a green field. Their motto is 'Proud to be Faithful'. During Aegon's Conquest, Stokeworth surrendered to Visenya Targaryen after Vhagar set ablaze the castle's roofs. Lord Alyn Stokeworth was named Hand of the King to Aegon I Targaryen in 34 AC. When King Aegon I Targaryen ordered the Aegonfort torn down and the Red Keep built in its place. When the realm began to turn against King Maegor I Targaryen for Prince Jaehaerys Targaryen, Lord Stokeworth was one of the few lords who gathered at his command. When the Dance of the Dragons begun, Lord Stokeworth was among the black lords at court imprisoned for being loyal to Princess Rhaenyra Targaryen instead of her rival, Aegon II Targaryen. When brought before the King's Justice, however, Lord Stokeworth was one of the few lords to swear fealty to Aegon. He was later beheaded by Queen Rhaenyra for this betrayal. Despite this House Stokeworth persisted onwards.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_5}House Darklyn of Duskendale
Faction.text: House Darklyn of Duskendale is a noble house who rules the town of Duskendale and the surrounding lands in the Crownlands. Their keep is the Dun Fort, at the top of a hill that dominates the town. The Darklyns blazoned their arms with fusily black and gold, seven white shields upon a red tierce. The seven white shields are for the seven Darklyns who were knights of the Kingsguard. The Darklyns were petty kings of the First Men during the Age of Heroes. During the coming of the Andals, the Darklyns forced Togarion Bar Emmon to flee from the northern shore of the Blackwater to Massey's Hook. During Aegon's Conquest, Aegon I Targaryen and his bastard half-brother Orys Baratheon defeated the Darklyns of Duskendale and the Mootons of Maidenpool in Aegon's first test. The Darklyns bent the knee to the Targaryens and served their new kings as the Lords of Duskendale. Ser Robin Darklyn, called Darkrobin, was among the first seven knights of the Kingsguard. Seven Darklyns eventually joined the Kingsguard, more than any other house. When Prince Aegon Targaryen warred against King Maegor I Targaryen, Ser Davos Darklyn of the Kingsguard led five thousand men from King's Landing in support of the king during the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye. Davos was slain during the battle by Lord Qarl Corbray. Davos's aged father was slain when Ser Joffrey Doggett ordered Warrior's Sons to hunt Maegor's loyalists. When the realm began to turn against Maegor for Prince Jaehaerys Targaryen, Lord Darklyn was among the score of lesser lords who gathered at his command. Ser Steffon Darklyn was a member of the Kingsguard of King Viserys I Targaryen. When the Dance of the Dragons began, pitting the blacks of Rhaenyra Targaryen against the greens of her half-brother Aegon II Targaryen, Steffon became Lord Commander of Rhaenyra's Queensguard. However, Steffon died attempting to tame Seasmoke during the Sowing of the Seeds.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_6}House Buckwell of the Antlers
Faction.text: House Buckwell of Antlers rules over the Antlers. House Buckwell is a noble house in the Crownlands. When Aegon I Targaryen landed at the mouth of Blackwater Bay, which would later be known as King’s Landing, Lord Buckwell swore allegiance to the Conqueror. House Buckwell is now sworn to King Viserys I Targaryen of House Targaryen. When the realm began to turn against King Maegor I Targaryen for Prince Jaehaerys Targaryen, Lord Buckwell was among the score of lesser lords who gathered at his command. They blazon their arms with a rack of golden antlers on vair. Their words are 'Pride and Purpose.'

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_7}House Thorne
Faction.text: House Thorne is a noble house from the Crownlands. They blazon their shields with a silver flail on red within a black border rayonne. Ser Rickard Thorne was a knight of the Kingsguard during the reign of Viserys I Targaryen. During the Dance of the Dragons, Ser Rickard was charged with protecting Prince Maelor Targaryen, the surviving son and heir of Aegon II Targaryen, but both the knight and prince were slain by a mob at Bitterbridge.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_8}House Chelsted
Faction.text: House Chelsted is a noble house from the crownlands. They blazon their arms with per bend green and white, a crossed spiked mace and a silver dagger.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_9}House Stauntom of Rook's Rest
Faction.text: House Staunton of Rook's Rest is a noble house from Rook's Rest in the Crownlands. When the realm began to turn against King Maegor I Targaryen for Prince Jaehaerys Targaryen, Lord Staunton was among the score of lesser lords who gathered at his command. The Stauntons supported the blacks and Princess Rhaenyra Targaryen's claim to the Iron Throne during the Dance of the Dragons. Lord Staunton was killed by the greens during the battle at Rook's Rest, and his head was taken to King's Landing. They blazon their arms with two black wings upon a white fess on checkered black and grey.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_10}House Brune of Dyre Den
Faction.text: House Brune of the Dyre Den is a noble house from the Dyre Den, which is located in Crackclaw Point in the crownlands. The Brunes of Brownhollow, a family of landed knights, are cousins to the Brunes of the Dyre Den. The Brothers Brune were legendary champions who brought peace to Crackclaw Point. Bernarr Brune was squire to Lord Alyn Stokeworth, the Hand of the King to King Aegon I and Aenys I Targaryen. When Lord Alyn was slain by the rebel Harren the Red, Bernarr cut down the outlaw and was rewarded with knighthood. Bernarr was later one of the champions in Maegor I Targaryen's trial of seven. During the Dance of the Dragons, the Brunes declared for Rhaenyra Targaryen. Brune forces joined Lord Walys Mooton to liberate Rook's Rest from the control of Aegon II Targaryen's greens. Lord Brune marched from Maidenpool to King's Landing with Lord Manfryd Mooton, Lord Leowyn Corbray, and Ser Rennifer Crabb after Aegon's death. They blazon their arms with a bear paw, white on brown, within a double tressure white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_11}House Brune of Brownhollow
Faction.text: House Brune of the Dyre Den is a noble house from the Dyre Den, which is located in Crackclaw Point in the crownlands. The Brunes of Brownhollow, a family of landed knights, are cousins to the Brunes of the Dyre Den. The Brothers Brune were legendary champions who brought peace to Crackclaw Point. Bernarr Brune was squire to Lord Alyn Stokeworth, the Hand of the King to King Aegon I and Aenys I Targaryen. When Lord Alyn was slain by the rebel Harren the Red, Bernarr cut down the outlaw and was rewarded with knighthood. Bernarr was later one of the champions in Maegor I Targaryen's trial of seven. During the Dance of the Dragons, the Brunes declared for Rhaenyra Targaryen. Brune forces joined Lord Walys Mooton to liberate Rook's Rest from the control of Aegon II Targaryen's greens. Lord Brune marched from Maidenpool to King's Landing with Lord Manfryd Mooton, Lord Leowyn Corbray, and Ser Rennifer Crabb after Aegon's death. They blazon their arms with a bear paw, brown on white, within a double tressure brown.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_12}House Celtigar of Claw Isle
Faction.text: House Celtigar of Claw Isle rules Claw Isle, an island in the Narrow Sea off the coast of Crackclaw Point. House Celtigar is an ancient and proud house, with the blood of old Valyria in its veins. The Celtigars claim dominion over Crackclaw Point, but the inhabitants do not acknowledge their claim. Historically, House Celtigar is an ancient and proud house, with the blood of old Valyria in its veins. Crispian Celtigar served as master of coin to King Aegon I Targaryen. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Bartimos Celtigar sat on the black council as master of coin to Queen Rhaenyra Targaryen. The Celtigars' castle is rumored to hold fabulous treasures that bear witness to their wealth. Among House Celtigar's property there are Myrish carpets, Volantene glass, gold and silver plate, jeweled cups, magnificent hawks, a trained sea eagle, an axe of Valyrian steel, chests of rubies, a horn said to summon krakens from under the sea, and many fine wines. Their arms depict red crabs strewn on white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_13}House Strickland
Faction.text: House Strickland is a noble house in from the Crownlands. Their lands are near the Blackwater Rush.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_14}House Chyttering
Faction.text: House Chyttering is a noble house of the Crownlands sworn to King's Landing. Their arms depict three golden bendlets enhanced on white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_15}House Farring
Faction.text: House Farring is a noble house of the Crownlands. They blazon their arms with per pale purple and white, two knights combatant with swords, counter-charged.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_16}House Rambton
Faction.text: House Rambton is a noble house from the Crownlands. They blazon their arms with a white ram's head with golden horns on red.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_17}House Sunglass of Sweetport Sound
Faction.text: House Sunglass of Sweetport Sound is a noble house from the Crownlands. Their blazon is composed by seven golden seven-pointed stars in a ring on white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_18}House Massey of Stonedance
Faction.text: House Massey of Stonedance is a noble house from Stonedance on Massey's Hook in the Crownlands. The Masseys are a house of First Men origin. During the Age of the Hundred Kingdoms, petty kings from House Massey were one of many who historically claimed the mouth of the Blackwater Rush. When Aegon the Conqueror launched his invasion of Westeros, the Masseys supported him instead of the Storm King, Argilac the Arrogant. The master of laws for King Aegon I was Triston Massey. With the creation of the crownlands after the Conquest, the Masseys became sworn to the Targaryens of Aegon's new capital, King's Landing, instead of House Baratheon of Storm's End, who had replaced the Durrandons. Lord Lucifer Massey stood for King Maegor I Targaryen in his trial of seven, and was killed by the Warrior's Son Ser Harys Horpe. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Gormon Massey was killed attempting to tame the dragon Vermithor on Dragonstone during the Sowing of the Seeds. Elinda Massey was a lady-in-waiting for Queen Rhaenyra Targaryen, and reportedly gouged out her own eyes at the sight of her queen being devoured by the dragon Sunfyre. They blazon their arms with a triple spiral; red, green and blue, on white, an ancient sigil for an ancient House.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_19}House Bar Emmon of Sharp Point
Faction.text: House Bar Emmon of Sharp Point is a noble house from Sharp Point in the Crownlands. They are sworn directly to Dragonstone. Togarion Bar Emmon was an Andal warlord who founded a small kingdom north of the Blackwater Rush during the Andal invasion, but he was opposed by the more powerful Darklyn kings of Duskendale. Sensing a weakness to the south, Togarion married the daughter of King Josua Massey of Stonedance, himself a rival to the Storm King Qarlton II Durrandon, and the Bar Emmons abandoned the Blackwater for Massey's Hook. Prior to the War of Conquest, House Bar Emmon was sworn to House Durrandon, the Storm Kings from Storm's End, but had closer ties to House Targaryen of nearby Dragonstone. When the Seven Kingdoms began to turn against King Maegor I Targaryen for his nephew Prince Jaehaerys, Lord Bar Emmon was among the score of lesser lords who gathered at his command. Lord Bar Emmon was a member of the black council of Queen Rhaenyra Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons. Their blazon is a leaping blue swordfish on fretty silver on white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_20}House Bywater
Faction.text: House Bywater is a Noble House from the Crownlands located along the shore of the God's Eye. When the realm began to turn against King Maegor I Targaryen for Prince Jaehaerys Targaryen, Lord Bywater was one of the few lords who gathered at his command. However, when Maegor mysteriously died upon the Iron Throne, Lord Bywater fled to his seat. They blazon their shields with fretty blue on white, three silver fish on a blue chief.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_21}House Hardy
Faction.text: House Hardy is a noble house from the crownlands. Their lands are located on Crackclaw Point. Lord Lucifer Hardy is remembered as being a champion who brought peace to Crackclaw Point, but the peace did not last past his lifetime. A knight from House Hardy once served in the Kingsguard.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_22}House Pyne
Faction.text: House Pyne is a noble house from the Crownlands. Their lands are located on Crackclaw Point. A member of House Pyne once served in the Kingsguard.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_23}House Scales
Faction.text: House Scales of Dragonstone is a noble house of the Crownlands, sworn to House Targaryen of Dragonstone. Counted among the lesser houses of Dragonstone, they hold their place in service to the Targaryens, maintaining their loyalty to the ancient seat of the dragonlords and the domain surrounding Blackwater Bay.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_24}House Hogg of Sow's Horn
Faction.text: House Hogg of Sow’s Horn is a noble house of the Crownlands, seated at Sow’s Horn and sworn to the Iron Throne. They bear for arms a brown boar upon a white field. Counted among the lesser houses of the Crownlands, they have served the crown across the reigns of the Targaryen kings, with Jon Hogg fighting for King Maegor I at the Battle at the Great Fork before later being slain by the Warrior’s Sons. In the early years of King Jaehaerys I, Ser Harys Hogg was well regarded in tourneys and a favorite of the smallfolk, while in later years Priscella Hogg, a companion to Queen Daenaera Velaryon, was implicated in a poisoning plot within the royal household and condemned to mutilation, her sentence to be set aside should she take vows with the Faith.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_25}House Cargyll
Faction.text: House Cargyll is a noble house of the Crownlands, sworn to the Iron Throne. They bear for arms a golden goose upon a bendy sinister field of black and red. Counted among the lesser houses of the Crownlands, they are chiefly remembered for the twin brothers Ser Arryk and Ser Erryk Cargyll of the Kingsguard, who chose opposing sides during the Dance of the Dragons, Arryk for King Aegon II and Erryk for Princess Rhaenyra, and slew one another in single combat during the war.

Faction.name: {=LOC_CROWN_26}House Hollard
Faction.text: House Hollard is a noble house of the Crownlands, sworn to House Darklyn of Duskendale. They bear for arms barry red and pink, with three golden crowns upon a blue chief. In the Age of Heroes, the Hollards were among the closest allies of House Darklyn, with three daughters of their line wed to Darklyn kings, binding the two houses closely together in the early history of Duskendale and its rule. Their seat is northeast of Duskendale.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_1}House Baratheon
Faction.text: House Baratheon of Storm's End is one of the Great Houses of Westeros, and is the principal house in the stormlands, which they rule as Lords Paramount of the Stormlands. Their seat, Storm's End, is an ancient castle raised by the Storm Kings from the now-extinct House Durrandon. House Baratheon is the youngest of the Great Houses of Westeros. It was created when Orys Baratheon, one of Aegon I Targaryen's generals, his closest companion, and his rumored bastard half-brother, took Argella Durrandon, the only daughter of Argilac Durrandon, the last Storm King, to wife. Orys took the sigil and words of House Durrandon for his own and became the first Lord of Storm's End. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Borros Baratheon sided the House with Aegon II Targaryen and the greens, despite his late father, Lord Boremund's support of the blacks. Members of the family tend to be tall and powerfully built, with black hair and blue eyes, as well as strong, square jawlines. They are known for their mercurial tempers. The Baratheon sigil is a crowned black stag on a field of gold,and their words are 'Ours is the Fury'

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_2}House Wensington
Faction.text: House Wensington is a noble house from the stormlands. As the upper part of the Wensington coat of arms shows the stag of House Baratheon and House Durrandon. Their arms are two golden trumpets crossed on blue, a gold chief with three black stags. Their motto is 'Sound The Charge'.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_3}House Errol of Haystack Hall
Faction.text: House Errol of Haystack Hall is a noble house from Haystack Hall in the stormlands, one of the principal houses sworn to House Baratheon. During Aegon's Conquest, Lords Errol, Fell, and Buckler ambushed Orys Baratheon's host as he crossed the Wendwater. The lords later hid in the kingswood, but Rhaenys Targaryen set the forests ablaze with Meraxes. Lord Errol was killed prior to the Last Storm. They blazon their shield as a yellow haystack on orange.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_4}House Buckler of Bronzegate
Faction.text: House Buckler of Bronzegate is a noble house from Bronzegate in the Stormlands sworn to Storm's End. During Aegon's Conquest, Lords Buckler, Errol, and Fell opposed Orys Baratheon during his march from the Aegonfort toward Storm's End, but the storm lords were forced to retreat when Rhaenys Targaryen had Meraxes set afire parts of what is now the southern kingswood. At the beginning of the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Buckler was executed in King's Landing for refusing to renounce his oaths to Rhaenyra Targaryen. Their arms depict three brass buckles on blue.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_5}House Fell of Felwood
Faction.text: House Fell of Felwood is a noble house sworn to Storm's End. Their seat is Felwood in the northern Stormlands. During Aegon's Conquest, Lords Fell, Errol, and Buckler ambushed Orys Baratheon's host as he crossed the Wendwater. The lords later hid in the kingswood, but Rhaenys Targaryen set the forests ablaze with Meraxes.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_6}House Cafferen of Fawnton
Faction.text: House Cafferen of Fawnton is a noble house from Fawnton in the stormlands, sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End. Ser Jon Cafferen, heir to Fawnton during the First Dornish War, was gelded by the Wyl of Wyl during his wedding to Alys Oakheart. They blazon their arms with two white fawns counter salient on green.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_7}House Penrose of Parchments
Faction.text: House Penrose of Parchments is a noble house of the Stormlands, sworn to House Baratheon of Storm’s End. House Penrose has a storied past, marked by close ties to House Targaryen. Lord Ronnel Penrose married Princess Elaena Targaryen and serves as master of coin on King Daeron II Targaryen’s small council, though Elaena was widely credited with fulfilling the role’s duties. Aelinor Penrose later wed Prince Aerys Targaryen. Their seat, Parchments, reflects their dedication to record-keeping and intellect, symbolized by their blazon: brunâtre, two crossed argent quills. Their words, 'Set Down Our Deeds', capture their legacy of preserving history and honoring their accomplishments.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_8}House Tarth of Evenfall Hall
Faction.text: House Tarth of Evenfall Hall is a noble house from Evenfall Hall in the Stormlands. The family rules Tarth, a beautiful island northeast of Shipbreaker Bay that controls the Straits of Tarth between the island and mainland Westeros. They are one of the main houses sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End. Ancestors of House Tarth were once kings, and the head of the family is known as the Evenstar. The island of Tarth came under the dominion of Storm Kings of House Durrandon when Durran the Fair married the daughter of Tarth's king, Edwyn Evenstar. Tarth was the first region of the stormlands conquered during the coming of the Andals, but peace between the Andals and First Men was eventually achieved when the Storm Kings and their bannermen intermarried with the Andals. During the reign of Jaehaerys I Targaryen, Lord Cameron Tarth requested help from the Iron Throne when Tarth was invaded by Myrish exiles in 92 AC, following the Myrish Bloodbath. Lord Tarth attended the Great Council at Harrenhal in 101 AC. At the start of the Dance of the Dragons in 129 AC, Ser Otto Hightower believed that the Evenstar of Tarth would be among the narrow sea powers who would support Rhaenyra Targaryen. In 133 AC during the Daughters' War, Lord Bryndemere Tarth supplied twelve longships to the fleet of Ser Gedmund Peake. Their blazon is quartered with yellow suns on rose and white crescent moons on azure.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_9}House Rogers of Amberly
Faction.text: House Rogers of Amberly is a noble house from Amberly in the Stormlands. They blazon their arms with nine unicorns around a maze, silver on black.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_10}House Wylde of the Rain House
Faction.text: House Wylde of the Rain House is a noble house from the Rain House in the Stormlands. It is one of the principal houses sworn to House Baratheon. Lord Jasper Wylde was the master of laws during the reign of Viserys I Targaryen and the civil war known as the Dance of the Dragons, in which he supported the greens. He was executed as a traitor by Rhaenyra Targaryen when she took King's Landing in 130 AC. House Wylde blazon their arms with a blue-green maelstrom on gold.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_11}House Estermont of Greenstone
Faction.text: House Estermont of Greenstone is a noble house from Greenstone in the Stormlands and is one of the principal houses sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End. They rule the small island of Estermont east of Cape Wrath. It is as yet unconfirmed if the Estermonts are of First Men or Andal ancestry. During the coming of the Andals, the Storm Kings from House Durrandon and many of their bannermen intermarried with Andals. Their arms depict a dark green sea turtle on pale green.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_12}House Whitehead of Weeping Town
Faction.text: House Whitehead of the Weeping Town is a noble house from the Weeping Town, a market town in the stormlands. Lying on the southern shore of Cape Wrath, along the Sea of Dorne, it has a thriving port which handles much of the stormlands' trade. It contains a seat, known as the Weeping Tower. The town has stout wooden walls, peaked roofs, and crooked alleys. It is large enough to support three inns: the Broken Shield, the Loon, and the Drunken Dornishman. It is known as the Weeping Town because it is where the corpse of Daeron I Targaryen was first brought after the king was murdered in Dorne, remaining there for three days.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_13}House Mertyns of Mistwood
Faction.text: House Mertyns is a noble house from the Stormlands and are sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End. Their seat, Mistwood, is a castle located along the coast of Shipbreaker Bay. During the First Dornish War, Lord Mertyns and his entire household were poisoned by means of a cask of Dornish wine by Dornish assassins. They blazon their arms with a white great horned owl on grey.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_14}House Connington of Griffin's Roost
Faction.text: House Connington of Griffin's Roost is a lordly house from Griffin's Roost in the Stormlands whose lands extend many leagues west, north, and south of Griffin's Roost, including reaching near to the Rainwood. During the First Dornish War, Lord Connington was killed by Dornish assassins while hunting. The fourth son of the ruling Lord Connington joined Prince Aegon the Uncrowned's forces in his campaign against his uncle King Maegor the Cruel and fought for him in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye. Lord Kyle Connington was a guest at the Golden Wedding. The young Lord Roy Connington became a companion of Princess Saera Targaryen during his stay at court. After being involved in a sexual scandal, Roy was condemned to a ten-year exile from Westeros by Saera's father, King Jaehaerys I Targaryen. With the death of Lord Borros Baratheon during the Dance of the Dragons and his heir being an infant, the Stormlands became vulnerable to Dornish incursions. Borros's widow, Lady Elenda Baratheon, married Ser Steffon Connington hoping the fierce warrior would fight off the Dornishmen and bring stability to the region. Their blazon shows two griffins combatant counterchanged per pale on red and white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_15}House Swann of Stonehelm
Faction.text: House Swann of Stonehelm is one of the primary noble houses of the Stormlands. They are marcher lords ruling from Stonehelm over the lands surrounding the Red Watch on Cape Wrath in the eastern Dornish Marches. House Swann is an old noble house, regarded as proud, powerful and cautious. They have governed their lands for as long as any can remember. From Stonehelm they control the Slayne, a major river route inland to the Stormlands, gaining them much wealth and power. They are probably the second most powerful and influential family in the Stormlands, after the Baratheons. The Swanns and Carons both claim to be the oldest of the marcher lords. Some time after driving away the pirate presence on the Stepstones in 96 AC, When her infamously niggardly uncle refused to pay the ransom, she was sold to a pillow house. Johanna became a celebrated courtesan known as the Black Swan, the eventual ruler of Lys in all but name. During the Dance of the Dragons, Ser Byron Swann was killed by Syrax or Vhagar. They blazon their arms with argent and sable a swan counter charged.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_16}House Staedmon of Broad Arch
Faction.text: House Staedmon of Broad Arch is one of the noble houses from the Stormlands sworn to the Baratheons of Storm's End. Their castle is Broad Arch. They blazon the arms as a black dagger piercing a red heart on white de sang within an undulating red border.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_17}House Dondarrion of Blackhaven
Faction.text: House Dondarrion is a noble house from the Stormlands. Their seat, Blackhaven, is a castle located in the Dornish Marches near the Boneway, controlling the pass to Dorne. They are sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End. Their sigil is a forked purple lightning bolt on a black field, speckled with four-pointed stars.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_18}House Grandison of Grandview
Faction.text: House Grandison of Grandview is a noble house from Grandview in the Stormlands. They blazon their arms with a black sleeping lion on yellow. Their words are 'Rouse Me Not'

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_19}House Trant of Gallowsgrey
Faction.text: House Trant of Gallowsgrey is a noble family from the stormlands. They are one of the principal houses sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End. The seat of House Trant is Gallowsgrey and their words are 'So End Our Foes'. They blazon their shields with azure, a hanged man, sable.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_20}House Selmy of Harvest Hall
Faction.text: House Selmy of Harvest Hall is one of the principal noble houses from the Stormlands. Its seat is Harvest Hall in the Dornish Marches. They blazon their arms with three stalks of yellow wheat on brown.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_21}House Caron of Nightsong
Faction.text: House Caron of Nightsong is one of the principal noble houses in the Stormlands, sworn to Storm's End. They rule from Nightsong and hold the title Lord of the Marches, although they do not hold dominion over other marcher lords in the Dornish Marches. The Carons date back to the Age of Heroes, and they are a family known for creating warriors and singers. They claim their nightingale sigil has been in a thousand battles. During the First Dornish War, Lord Fowler burned Nightsong and took Lord Caron and his family captive. The first Vulture King besieged Nightsong with a host of Dornishmen, but was unable to capture the castle. After the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Royce Caron was chosen as one of the regents of King Aegon III Targaryen, but he resigned a year later. Their blazon is a field of black nightingales on yellow. Their words are 'No Song so Sweet'.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_22}House Morrigen of Crow's Nest
Faction.text: House Morrigen of Crow's Nest is a noble house from Crow's Nest in the Stormlands. Dickon Morrigen was killed alongside the Bastard of Blackhaven by Meraxes during the Last Storm. Ser Damon the Devout, the captain of the Warrior's Sons, died during a trial of seven involving King Maegor I Targaryen. Ser Gyles Morrigen was a Lord Commander of the Kingsguard for Jaehaerys I Targaryen. Their arms are a black crow in flight on storm-green.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_23}House Peasebury of Poddingfield
Faction.text: House Peasebury of Poddingfield is a noble house from Poddingfield in the Stormlands. They blazon their arms with a pea pod burst open, green on white, bordered by three rows of green peas

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_24}House Hasty
Faction.text: House Hasty is a noble house of landed knights from the stormlands. They blazon their arms with a white bend cotised on purple.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_25}House Bolling
Faction.text: House Bolling is a noble house of landed knights from the stormlands, sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End. Ser Theo Bolling was a landed knight married to Lady Elinor Costayne. During the reign of King Maegor I Targaryen, Ser Theo fought for King Maegar in his campaign against the Poor Fellows. In 47 AC, Maegor had Ser Theo arrested, charged with treason, and executed on the same day, allowing Maegor to marry Elinor. Ser Theo's eldest sons were fostered at the Eyrie and Highgarden, while the youngest was given over to a wet nurse. Their arms are vairy orange and blue; upon a black canton, a golden stag beneath an orange bend sinister.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_26}House Horpe
Faction.text: House Horpe is a noble house from the stormlands. Ser Harys Horpe was one of the Warrior's Sons who were killed in the trial of seven of King Maegor I Targaryen. They blazon their arms with per chevron engrailed grey and bone, three death's head moths countercharged.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_27}House Gower
Faction.text: House Gower is a noble house of the Stormlands, seated at Gower, and they blazon their arms with nine yellow trefoils on a black cross on ermine. They are sworn to House Baratheon of Storm's End, owing fealty and military service as part of the stormlords in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion.

Faction.name: {=LOC_STORM_28}House Cole
Faction.text: House Cole is a noble house of the Dornish Marches in the Stormlands, sworn to House Dondarrion of Blackhaven. They blazon their arms with ten black pellets on scarlet, the pellets representing lumps of coal, from which the house takes its name. The most renowned member of the house is Ser Criston Cole, the son of the steward of Blackhaven, who rose to become Lord Commander of the Kingsguard and later Hand of the King to Aegon II Targaryen, playing a decisive role in crowning him and helping ignite the Dance of the Dragons, earning the name “Kingmaker” before his death at the Butcher's Ball.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_1}House Tully of Riverrun
Faction.text: House Tully of Riverrun is one of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms. Their sigil is a silver trout leaping on a striped field of blue and mud red, and their house words are 'Family, Duty, Honor.' Lord Edmure Tully, the Lord Paramount of the Trident, rules over the Riverlands from the Tully seat of Riverrun. House Tully follows the Faith of the Seven, although Riverrun still has a godswood. They are an old noble house of First Men origin, dating back to the Age of Heroes. Ser Edmure Tully and his sons were allies of Tristifer IV Mudd, the King of the Rivers and the Hills, but the Tullys knelt to the Andal conqueror Armistead Vance after Tristifer's death. Edmure's son, Axel Tully, received land at the confluence of the Red Fork and the Tumblestone, where he constructed Riverrun.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_2}House Grell
Faction.text: House Grell is a noble house from the riverlands. They blazon their arms with three red martlets on a white bend, on blue.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_3}House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest
Faction.text: House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest is one of the two great branches of House Vance in the Riverlands, the other being House Vance of Atranta. They blazon their arms as quartered, a black dragon on white and two golden eyes in a golden ring on black. Armistead Vance was the mightiest of the Andal conquerors who defeated Tristifer IV Mudd, King of the Rivers and the Hills, during the coming of the Andals to the Riverlands. In the century after the downfall of House Justman, the Vances were among the petty kings who disputed the Riverlands. The Vances joined the rebellion of river lords against Harren Hoare, King of the Isles and the Rivers, during Aegon's Conquest. They also supported the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_4}House Piper of Pinkmaiden
Faction.text: House Piper of Pinkmaiden is one of the chief noble houses from the Riverlands. Its seat at Pinkmaiden Castle is located on the Red Fork close to the border with the Westerlands. Their blazon is a pink dancing maiden in a swirl of white silk, over blue. During Aegon's Conquest, the Pipers joined Aegon the Conqueror against Harren the Black. Melony Piper was a favorite of the young Princess Rhaena Targaryen. When Prince Aegon Targaryen rose against King Maegor I Targaryen, the host of Aegon and Rhaena gathered at Pinkmaiden Castle, the home of Melony's brother, Lord Jon Piper. Melony was killed in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye in 43 AC. Rhaena and her dragon, Dreamfyre, briefly stayed at Pinkmaiden with the Pipers in 51 AC. Lord Petyr Piper declared for the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons, and the Pipers aided in the capture of Stone Hedge. Petyr knighted Pate of Longleaf after the Battle of the Red Fork. Petyr and his favorite grandson died fighting westermen. Lord Stanton Piper was burned alongside Lord Lyonel Deddings by Vermithor during the Second Battle of Tumbleton. Members of House Piper tend to have red hair.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_5}House Smallwood of Acorn Hall
Faction.text: House Smallwood of Acorn Hall is a noble house from Acorn Hall in the Riverlands. Their sigil is six brown acorns on yellow. The lands of House Smallwood are near those that belonged to House Goodbrook. Lord Smallwood died fighting in King Arlan III Durrandon's campaign against King Humfrey I Teague, which included the Battle of Six Kings. During the reign of King Aenys I Targaryen, Lord Myles Smallwood fought with Lord Alyn Stokeworth in his campaign against Harren the Red. During the Faith Militant uprising, Lord Smallwood battled Wat the Hewer in the battle at Stonebridge. He later sided with Prince Aegon the Uncrowned in the Battle Beneath the Gods Eye against his uncle, King Maegor I Targaryen.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_6}House Bracken of Stone Hedge
Faction.text: House Bracken of Stone Hedge is an old house from Stone Hedge in the Riverlands, one of the main families sworn to House Tully of Riverrun. They blazon their arms with a red stallion upon a golden escutcheon, on brown. The Bracken lands lie along the Red Fork of the Trident. They rule over wider domains and can field a much larger army than their overlord of House Tully. The Brackens have the blood of the First Men in their veins and were kings of the Riverlands during the Age of Heroes. They have an old and bitter feud against their neighbors, the Blackwoods. According to the Brackens, they were kings who ruled the Red Fork but were usurped by their vassals, the Blackwoods. According to the Blackwoods, however, the Brackens were petty lords and horse breeders who hired sellswords to usurp the Blackwood Kings.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_7}House Shawney
Faction.text: House Shawney is a noble house from the Riverlands. They blazon their arms with per fess; blue, red, green above, a white catfish on black below. The upper colors represent the three forks of the Trident.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_8}House Harlton of Castlewood
Faction.text: House Harlton of Castlewood is a noble house from Castlewood in the riverlands. The family also owns a manse in King's Landing.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_9}House Vance of Atranta
Faction.text: House Vance of Atranta is one of the two great branches of House Vance in the Riverlands, the other being House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest. House Vance rules over wider domains and can field a much larger army than their liege lords, House Tully. They blazon their arms as quartered, a green dragon on white and a white tower on black. House Vance of Atranta supported the greens of Aegon II Targaryen during the Dance of the Dragons, while House Vance of Wayfarer's Rest declared for the blacks of Rhaenyra Targaryen. The greens in the Riverlands yielded after the taking of Stone Hedge.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_10}House Deddings of Stoney Sept
Faction.text: House Deddings is a noble house from the riverlands. It is a wealthy house. During the Faith Militant uprising, Visenya Targaryen used Vhagar to set aflame in a single night the seats of Houses Deddings, Blanetree, Lychester, Terrick, and Wayn. During the Dance of the Dragons, Davos Deddings was stabbed in the eye while trying to protect his sister, Lady Darla, from rapers in the riots of King's landing. Lords Stanton Piper and Lyonel Deddings were burned by Vermithor during the Second Battle of Tumbleton.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_11}House Ryger of Willow Wood
Faction.text: House Ryger of Willow Wood is a noble house from Willow Wood in the riverlands. It is one of the main houses sworn to House Tully. They blazon their arms with a weeping willow, green on white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_12}House Wayn
Faction.text: House Wayn is a noble house from the Riverlands. Lord Tristifer Wayn participated in the battle at the Great Fork during the Faith Militant uprising. Visenya Targaryen used Vhagar to set aflame in a single night the seats of Houses Wayn, Blanetree, Deddings, Lychester, and Terrick. They blazon their arms with four wheels countercharged on quartered field blue and white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_13}House Lychester
Faction.text: House Lychester is a noble house in the riverlands. In 42 AC, during the Faith Militant uprising, Lord Jon Lychester was among the pious lords who fought King Maegor I Targaryen in the battle at the Great Fork of the Blackwater. The following year, Visenya Targaryen mounted Vhagar to set aflame several keeps and castles, including the seat of House Lychester. They own a small square keep and they blazon their arms with orange and white gyronny, a black talon.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_14}House Darry
Faction.text: House Darry of Darry is a noble house from Castle Darry in the Riverlands. House Darry is one of the more prominent and powerful houses of the Trident. House Darry traces their rule to the days when the Andals cast down the First Men. During the coming of the Andals, three sons of Lord Darry held back the Andals of Vorian Vypren at the Widow's Ford before being slain. Their blazon is a black plowman on brown.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_15}House Lothston of Harrenhal
Faction.text: House Lothston of Harrenhal is a house from Harrenhal in the Riverlands. House Lothston is the sixth noble house to rule as Lords of Harrenhal after House Strong went extinct. They have held Harrenhal for generations, and are a powerful house of the Seven Kingdoms. In 42 AC, Ser Guy Lothston was slain defending Maegor I Targaryen during the king's Trial of Seven. Ser Lucas Lothston was the grandson of a hedge knight. In 135 AC, after the secret siege and the failed plot against King Aegon III Targaryen, Lucas was named master-at-arms of the Red Keep by Lord Torrhen Manderly to replace Ser Gareth Long. Lucas earned the respect of Prince Viserys Targaryen and his elder brother, King Aegon III. In 149 AC, Falena Stokeworth became the first mistress of Aegon IV Targaryen. The Bastard of Harrenhal was defeated by Ser Arlan of Pennytree in the melee at a tourney held at King's Landing in 193 AC. The Lothstons of Harrenhal had a bad reputation; Lord Lucas Lothston and his son Manfryd, for example, were renowned for their supposed practice of the black arts. Their arms are a black bat on a field divided bendwise, silver and gold.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_16}House Roote of Harroway
Faction.text: House Roote of Lord Harroway's Town is a noble house from Harroway in the riverlands. Ser Richard Roote was one of the first knights of the Kingsguard when the order was founded in 10 AC. Lord Roote supported Prince Aegon Targaryen in his unsuccessful war against King Maegor I Targaryen. House Roote had acquired Lord Harroway's Town by the start of the Dance of the Dragons, and they supported Rhaenyra Targaryen during the civil war. The Rootes helped to capture Stone Hedge, but Ser Criston Cole later defeated Lord Roote, Lord Darry, and Ser Oswald Wode along the Gods Eye and Prince Aemond Targaryen burned Harroway with Vhagar. They blazon their arms with a two-headed horse, brown, on a field of wavy green and green.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_17}House Mooton of Maidenpool
Faction.text: House Mooton of Maidenpool is a noble house of the Riverlands, sworn to House Tully. They are one of the largest port and trade centers in the Riverlands and Bay of Crabs. Their blazon is a red salmon on white, over a white field with a golden tressure and house words, 'Wisdom and Strength'. House Mooton is historically far wealthier than their overlord of House Tully. The Mootons were petty kings in the days of the First Men. The Storm King Monfryd the Mighty defeated a Mooton king and conquered Maidenpool prior to the coming of the Andals. King Florian the Brave was killed by Andals in the Fall of Maidenpool. The Mootons of Maidenpool were within the domains of Kings Benedict I Justman and King Torrence Teague, although the Riverlands eventually passed first to the Storm Kings under King Arlan III Durrandon and then to House Hoare under King Harwyn Hardhand.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_18}House Cox of Saltpans
Faction.text: House Cox of Saltpans is a noble house of landed knights from the Riverlands. Ser Quincy Cox is the Knight of Saltpans whom is responsible for protecting smallfolk. Saltpans is a town in the Riverlands that sits on the Bay of Crabs. Saltpans has never been an important trading port, but ships do call there from time to time. The castle of House Cox dominates the town; it is a small castle, no more than a holdfast, a single tall square keep with a bailey and a curtain wall. There are shops and inns and alehouses by the harbor, as well as a sept and a stable.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_19}House Butterwell of Whitewalls
Faction.text: House Butterwell is a noble house from Whitewalls in the Riverlands. House Butterwell is a rich house with extensive lands, though its main incomes came from dairy cows. House Butterwell is said to have the best wines outside of the Arbor. After King Maegor I Targaryen exterminated House Harroway in 44 AC, he divided the Harroway holdings among his remaining supporters. The king granted Lord Harroway's Town to Lord Alton Butterwell, Harrenhal to Lord Walton Towers, and the rest of the Harroway holdings to Lord Darnold Darry. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Butterwell initially supported Princess Rhaenyra Targaryen's claim to the Iron Throne and declared for the blacks. He was captured and sent to the Red Keep's dungeons. When he was faced with the choice between swearing fealty to Aegon II Targaryen or death, Lord Butterwell chose to defect to the greens. Lord Butterwell was Hand of the King to Aegon IV Targaryen. King Aegon stayed one night at Butterwell's castle, and gave his Hand a dragon egg in return for 'access' to his three maiden daughters. By the next day all three girls had royal bastards on the way. He had Whitewalls built, the pale castle was commonly called the Milkhouse by the smallfolk who lived near it. Its walls, keep, and towers were made of white stone quarried in the Vale of Arryn, and its floors and pillars were crafted from white marble veined with gold. The rafters were carved from the trunks of weirwoods. Lord Butterwell's grandson, Ambrose Butterwell, was master of coin under King Aegon IV Targaryen and is currently Hand of the King, for Daeron II Targaryen. Their blazon is undy green, white, and yellow.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_20}House Blackwood of Raventree Hall
Faction.text: House Blackwood of Raventree Hall is an old house from Raventree Hall in the Riverlands, one of the main families sworn to House Tully of Riverrun. They once ruled the Riverlands as kings during the Age of Heroes. The Blackwoods can field a much larger army than their liege lords. They also ruled the Wolfswood as kings during the Age of Heroes, until the Starks drove them out of the North.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_21}House Paege of Fairmarket
Faction.text: House Paege is a noble house of landed knights sworn to Riverrun in the Riverlands. They blazon their shields with twining red-and-white snakes on black. The Paeges supported Harwyn Hardhand, King of the Iron Islands, when he warred against the Storm King Arrec Durrandon. In 43 AC, Lord Paege supported Aegon the Uncrowned against his uncle, King Maegor I Targaryen.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_22}House Goodbrook
Faction.text: House Goodbrook is a noble house of the Riverlands. Lord Goodbrook supported Harwyn Hardhand, King of the Iron Islands, when he warred against the Storm King Arrec Durrandon. They blazon their arms a blue wavy bend on gold.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_23}House Charlton
Faction.text: House Charlton is a noble house from the riverlands. They blazon their arms with three sprigs of mistletoe, green and red, on gold within a green border

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_24}House Vypren
Faction.text: House Vypren is a noble house of the Riverlands. They blazon their arms with a black toad on a white lily pad, on green. During the Andal invasion of the Riverlands, the Andal warlord Vorian Vypren and his knights were held at bay at the Widow's Ford for a day and a night by three sons of Lord Darry before finally slaying the Darrys. Lord Vypren supported Harwyn Hardhand, King of the Iron Islands, when he warred against the Storm King Arrec Durrandon.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_25}House Haigh
Faction.text: House Haigh is a noble house from the Riverlands. Their arms are a black pitchfork on a golden bar, sinister on russet. The Haighs are vassals of House Frey.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_26}House Erenford
Faction.text: House Erenford is a noble house of the riverlands. It is a vassal house of House Frey. They blazon their arms with a golden heron, beaked and gammed black, standing with a silver fish in its beak, on pink.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_27}House Mallister of Seagard
Faction.text: House Mallister of Seagard is one of the most prominent noble houses from the Riverlands. Seagard is a fortress built to defend the coast against the reavers from the Iron Islands, and dates from before Aegon's Conquest. Their blazon is a silver eagle displayed, on a purple field. Their motto is 'Above the rest'. House Mallister has a prouder lineage than their liege lords, House Tully of Riverrun, the Lords Paramount of the Trident. The Mallisters conquered most of the Cape of Eagles from the ironborn during the reign of King Torgon Greyiron. In the century that followed the fall of House Justman, the Mallisters were one of the many River Lords who were petty River Kings, with House Teague eventually controlling the Riverlands.

Faction.name: {=LOC_RIVER_28}House Frey of the Crossing
Faction.text: House Frey of the Crossing is a noble house of the Riverlands. Their main seat is the Twins, a pair of castles on each bank of the northern Green Fork of the Trident that serves as a vital bridge across the river. The lord of the house is called the Lord of the Crossing. Their sigil is two blue towers united by a bridge on silver-grey, representing the Twins. The strategic location of the Twins has allowed the house to become quite wealthy and can field larger armies than House Tully.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_1}House Lannister of Casterly Rock
Faction.text: House Lannister rules the Westerlands from Casterly Rock, a vast fortress carved into a towering stone promontory overlooking the Sunset Sea. Beneath the Rock lies the great city of Lannisport, one of the largest ports in the realm and a center of trade, shipbuilding, and commerce. The strength of House Lannister rests upon the rich gold and silver mines of the Westerlands, which have made them one of the wealthiest houses in the Seven Kingdoms. Their sigil is a golden lion on a crimson field. House words: 'Hear Me Roar'. The Lannisters claim descent from Lann the Clever, a legendary trickster said to have taken Casterly Rock from the ancient House Casterly through cunning rather than battle. For thousands of years afterward the Lannisters ruled the Westerlands as Kings of the Rock, until King Loren Lannister bent the knee to Aegon I Targaryen after the Field of Fire during Aegon's Conquest. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Lannister remains the dominant power of the west, commanding wealthy lands and numerous bannermen, and standing among the most influential noble houses of the Seven Kingdoms.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_2}House Lannister of Lannisport
Faction.text: House Lannister of Lannisport is a cadet branch of the main House Lannister of Casterly Rock, descended from younger sons of the ruling line. They rule the prosperous port city of Lannisport, the largest city in the Westerlands and one of the greatest trading hubs in the Seven Kingdoms. Situated beneath the shadow of Casterly Rock, Lannisport thrives on commerce, shipbuilding, and the flow of goods from across the Sunset Sea. Though they share the golden lion of their greater house, the Lannisters of Lannisport stand as loyal bannermen to their kin who rule from the Rock. For generations this branch of the family has governed the city in the name of the Lords of Casterly Rock, maintaining order within its busy streets and harbor while overseeing its merchants, guilds, and shipyards. Their position reflects both the wealth of the Westerlands and the far-reaching influence of House Lannister. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, the Lannisters of Lannisport remain an important part of the western power structure, administering one of the richest and most active cities in the realm while serving their powerful cousins of the Rock.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_3}House Crakehall
Faction.text: House Crakehall rules from Crakehall, a formidable castle in the southern Westerlands near the borders of the Reach. Their lands are fertile and well suited for farming and livestock, making them one of the stronger landed houses sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. The Crakehalls are known for producing large and powerful warriors, and their banners bear a brindled boar on a brown field. House words: 'None So Fierce'. For generations House Crakehall has served as a loyal bannerman of the Lannisters, providing soldiers and support to the Lords of Casterly Rock in times of war. Their position along the southern edge of the Westerlands places them near the border with the Reach, giving their castle strategic importance in guarding the approaches into Lannister lands. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Crakehall remains a steadfast and respected house of the Westerlands, known for strength in battle and long loyalty to the lions of the Rock.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_4}House Sarsfield
Faction.text: House Sarsfield rules from Sarsfield, a castle in the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their lands lie among the rolling hills of the west and form part of the network of bannermen that support the power of the Lannisters. Their sigil depicts a green arrowhead on a white field, reflecting the martial character of the house and its association with skilled warriors and archers. House words: 'True to the Mark'. Though not counted among the greatest houses of the Westerlands, House Sarsfield has long remained a loyal vassal to the lions of Casterly Rock and contributes its strength to the armies of the west when called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Sarsfield stands among the landed nobility of the Westerlands, bound by fealty to House Lannister and the rule of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_5}House Swyft of Cornfield
Faction.text: House Swyft of Cornfield is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Cornfield, a prosperous castle located in the fertile lands of the western hills. The Swyfts are known for their wealth and close ties to the Lannisters, often serving the ruling house of the Westerlands in administrative and courtly roles. Their sigil depicts a blue rooster on a yellow field, a device that reflects the house name and is well known throughout the region. Throughout the history of the Westerlands the Swyfts remain loyal bannermen of the Lannisters and continue to hold their lands and influence during the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen leading into the events that precede the First Blackfyre Rebellion.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_6}House Kenning of Kayce
Faction.text: House Kenning of Kayce is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Kayce, a coastal stronghold overlooking the Sunset Sea, which gives the house a strong maritime character compared to many of their inland neighbors. The Kennings of Kayce are distinct from the ironborn Kennings of Harlaw and belong firmly to the political order of the Westerlands, serving as westermen lords under Lannister rule. Their sigil bears a black ship on an orange field, reflecting their seafaring identity and the importance of the western coast to their position. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Kenning remains one of the landed noble houses that help bind the western shores to the power of Casterly Rock.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_7}House Prester of Feastfires
Faction.text: House Prester of Feastfires is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Feastfires, a castle in the western hills of the region. The Prestors are among the landed bannermen of the Lannisters and have long served the rulers of the Westerlands in war and peace. Their sigil depicts a red ox on a white field, a device associated with the strength and stubborn resolve of the house. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Prester remains a loyal vassal of the Lannisters, holding their lands in the western domains and contributing to the strength of the Westerlands.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_8}House Farman of Faircastle
Faction.text: House Farman of Faircastle is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Faircastle on Fair Isle, an island in the Sunset Sea off the western coast of the Westerlands. Because of their island holdings, the Farmans are known for their seafaring tradition and maintain ships that patrol the western waters and guard the approaches to the coast. Their sigil bears a red ship on a golden field with a white border, reflecting the house’s strong maritime identity and long association with the seas west of the mainland. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Farman remains one of the principal coastal houses of the Westerlands, serving the Lannisters while safeguarding the western seas.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_9}House Westerling of the Crag
Faction.text: House Westerling of the Crag is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is the Crag, a coastal castle overlooking the Sunset Sea along the western shores of the Westerlands. The Westerlings are one of the older houses of the region and hold lands along the coast that place them among the important westermen lords beneath Lannister rule. Their sigil bears six seashells arranged on a silver field, reflecting the house's coastal domain and long association with the western sea. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Westerling remains a loyal bannerman of the Lannisters and continues to hold the Crag and its surrounding lands as part of the western domains.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_10}House Reyne of Castamere
Faction.text: House Reyne of Castamere is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Castamere, a wealthy stronghold built above rich silver mines in the western hills. The Reynes are counted among the most powerful bannermen of the Lannisters during the height of their influence, their wealth and strength rivaling many of the great houses of the region. Their sigil bears a red lion rampant on a silver field, a heraldic device that reflects both their power and their rivalry in symbolism with the golden lions of House Lannister. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Reyne stands as one of the most prominent houses of the Westerlands, ruling from Castamere and commanding considerable wealth from their mines.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_11}House Tarbeck
Faction.text: House Tarbeck of Tarbeck Hall is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Tarbeck Hall, a castle located in the western hills of the region. The Tarbecks are one of the landed noble houses beneath the Lannisters and hold their lands as part of the network of bannermen that support the rule of Casterly Rock throughout the Westerlands. Their sigil bears a silver tankard on a red field, a heraldic device long associated with the house. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Tarbeck remains one of the established westermen houses serving under Lannister authority.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_12}House Drox
Faction.text: House Drox of Drox Castle is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Drox Castle, located in the western hills of the region among the lands held by the Lannisters’ bannermen. The Droxes are one of the lesser noble houses of the Westerlands and serve the Lannisters as part of the wider network of westermen lords who support the rule of Casterly Rock. Their sigil bears a black unicorn on a red field. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Drox remains a minor but loyal house of the Westerlands, holding their lands under Lannister authority.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_13}House Marbrand
Faction.text: House Marbrand of Ashemark is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Ashemark, a castle located in the western hills of the region. The Marbrands are counted among the prominent houses of the Westerlands and have long served the Lannisters as trusted bannermen in both war and governance. Their sigil bears a burning tree on a smoke grey field, a heraldic device recognized throughout the lands of the west. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Marbrand remains one of the established and respected houses of the Westerlands, holding Ashemark and supporting the authority of Casterly Rock.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_14}House Lefford of Golden Tooth
Faction.text: House Lefford of the Golden Tooth is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is the Golden Tooth, a powerful fortress that guards the western entrance to the pass between the Westerlands and the Riverlands. Because of this strategic position, the Leffords are responsible for controlling one of the most important land routes into the domain of the Lannisters. Their sigil bears a golden sun on a sky blue field, a device recognized throughout the Westerlands. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Lefford remains an important bannerman house of the Lannisters, holding the Golden Tooth and guarding the eastern approaches to the Westerlands.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_15}House Greenfield
Faction.text: House Greenfield of Greenfield is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Greenfield, a castle located in the lands of the Westerlands among the Lannisters’ bannermen. The Greenfields are one of the lesser noble houses of the region and serve the rulers of Casterly Rock as part of the broader network of westermen lords who uphold Lannister authority throughout the kingdom. Their sigil bears a green field with a white tower. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Greenfield remains a minor but established house of the Westerlands, holding their lands under the rule of the Lannisters.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_16}House Jast
Faction.text: House Jast of Jast is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is the castle of Jast, located within the lands of the Westerlands among the Lannisters’ bannermen. The Jasts are one of the lesser noble houses of the region and serve the rulers of Casterly Rock as part of the network of westermen lords who uphold Lannister authority throughout the kingdom. Their sigil bears a purple field with a golden lion’s paw. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Jast remains an established house of the Westerlands, holding their lands in service to the Lannisters.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_17}House Brax of Hornvale
Faction.text: House Brax of Hornvale is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Hornvale, a castle located in the hills of the western lands. The Braxes are one of the prominent houses of the Westerlands and have long served the Lannisters as loyal bannermen. Their sigil bears a purple unicorn on a silver field, a heraldic device well known throughout the west. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Brax remains one of the established houses of the Westerlands, holding Hornvale and supporting the authority of Casterly Rock.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_18}House Lydden of Deep Down
Faction.text: House Lydden of Deep Den is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock.
