Their seat is Deep Den, a strong castle located near the eastern approaches of the Westerlands along the road that leads toward the Riverlands. House Lydden claims descent from the First Men and their arms bear a badger on a field of yellow and brown. During the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen and in the years leading up to the First Blackfyre Rebellion, House Lydden remains one of the longstanding bannermen of the Lannisters, holding Deep Den and guarding the routes that lead into the western lands.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_19}House Westford
Faction.text: House Westford is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Though not counted among the greater houses of the region, they form part of the network of landed nobility that supports the authority of the Lannisters across the western hills and valleys. Like many Westerlands houses, their strength is tied to the wealth and resources of the region and the military obligations owed to the Lords of Casterly Rock. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Westford stands among the lesser houses of the Westerlands, bound by fealty to House Lannister and expected to provide men and service when the banners of the west are called.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_20}House Serrett of Silverhill
Faction.text: House Serrett rules from Silverhill, a castle in the western Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their lands lie among the hills and mining regions that contribute to the wealth of the west, and the house holds a respected position among the bannermen of the Lannisters. Their sigil bears a peacock displayed, and their colors are silver and blue. House words: 'I Have No Rival'. House Serrett has long served the Lords of Casterly Rock with loyalty, providing knights and soldiers to the armies of the Westerlands when called upon. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Serrett stands as one of the established noble houses of the region, holding Silverhill and its surrounding lands under the authority of House Lannister and the rule of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_21}House Estren of Wyndhall
Faction.text: House Estren rules from Wyndhall, a castle in the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their lands lie within the fertile western hills that support the power and prosperity of the region, and they serve as one of the many noble houses bound to the Lannisters through fealty and service. Their sigil depicts a gyronny of eight red and gold, reflecting their allegiance to the colors long associated with the lords of the west. Though not counted among the most powerful bannermen, House Estren maintains its place among the landed nobility of the Westerlands and contributes men and arms when the Lannisters call their banners. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Estren continues to hold Wyndhall and its surrounding lands under the authority of House Lannister and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_22}House Yarwyck
Faction.text: House Yarwyck is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Though little is recorded of their lands or stronghold, they stand among the many lesser houses that form the feudal structure supporting Lannister rule across the region. Their position reflects the layered hierarchy of the Westerlands, where numerous smaller houses provide service, soldiers, and loyalty to the Lords of Casterly Rock. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Yarwyck remains part of this network of bannermen, bound by fealty to House Lannister and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_23}House Peckledon
Faction.text: House Peckledon is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Though not counted among the principal houses of the region, they stand among the landed nobility that support Lannister authority across the hills and fertile lands of the west. Like many of the lesser houses of the Westerlands, their duties include providing men and service when the banners of the Rock are called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Peckledon remains one of the minor houses bound by fealty to House Lannister, contributing to the strength and stability of the western realm under the rule of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_24}House Payne
Faction.text: House Payne is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their lands lie within the domains of the west where they serve as loyal bannermen to the Lords of Casterly Rock. The house bears a purple field charged with silver coins, a sigil that reflects the wealth and commerce long associated with the Westerlands. Though not among the greatest houses of the region, House Payne has maintained its place within the feudal structure of the west, providing service and soldiers when the Lannisters call their banners. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Payne stands among the established noble houses of the Westerlands, bound by fealty to House Lannister and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_25}House Sarwyck of Riverspring
Faction.text: House Sarwyck is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their seat is Riverspring, a holding situated at the source of the Blackwater Rush, where the river begins its long course eastward toward the Crownlands and Blackwater Bay. The position of Riverspring gives the house a modest but notable place among the lesser nobility of the west, guarding the headwaters of one of the great rivers of the realm. Though not counted among the greater houses of the Westerlands, House Sarwyck remains bound in fealty to the Lords of Casterly Rock, owing military service and loyalty when the banners of the west are called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Sarwyck continues to hold Riverspring and its surrounding lands under the authority of House Lannister and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_26}House Plumm
Faction.text: House Plumm is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Though their lands and seat are not clearly recorded in surviving histories, they stand among the established bannermen of the Lannisters and have long served the Lords of Casterly Rock. Their sigil depicts a purple plumm on a gold field. House words: 'Come Try Me'. House Plumm also possesses a recent connection to House Targaryen, as the line descends in part from Elaena Targaryen, daughter of King Aegon III Targaryen, whose descendants married into the Plumm family. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Plumm remains part of the network of Westerlands nobility that supports Lannister rule and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_27}House Lorch
Faction.text: House Lorch is a noble house of the Westerlands sworn to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. Their lands lie within the domains of the west where they serve among the many bannermen that uphold Lannister authority. The house bears a black manticore on a gold field. Though not counted among the most powerful houses of the Westerlands, House Lorch has long maintained its place within the feudal structure of the region, providing service and soldiers when the banners of the Rock are called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Lorch stands among the established noble houses of the Westerlands, bound by fealty to House Lannister and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Westerlands_28}House Banefort
Faction.text: House Banefort rules from the Banefort, an ancient coastal fortress in the northwestern Westerlands overlooking the Sunset Sea. The castle is counted among the oldest strongholds in the region, with legends claiming it was raised in the Age of Heroes. Their sigil bears a hooded figure on a grey field, and their house is often associated with old tales and superstition that linger along the western coast. Though not among the most powerful bannermen of the west, House Banefort has long held its lands in service to House Lannister of Casterly Rock. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, the Baneforts remain a loyal house of the Westerlands, guarding the rocky shores of the Sunset Sea and providing support to the Lords of Casterly Rock when the banners of the west are called.

Faction.name: {=LOC_Reach_1}House Tyrell of Highgarden
Faction.text: House Tyrell rules the fertile lands of the Reach from Highgarden, a great castle that stands beside the Mander amid some of the richest farmland in the Seven Kingdoms. Though the Tyrells were once stewards to the ancient House Gardener, their fortunes changed during Aegon's Conquest when King Mern IX Gardener and his heirs perished at the Field of Fire. After the fall of the Gardener kings, Aegon I Targaryen raised House Tyrell to lordship over the Reach, granting them Highgarden and naming them Lords Paramount of the region. Their sigil bears a golden rose on a green field. House words: 'Growing Strong'. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Tyrell stands as one of the most powerful houses of the realm, ruling over the vast and fertile Reach and commanding the loyalty of numerous wealthy bannermen such as House Hightower, House Redwyne, House Rowan, House Tarly, and House Florent.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_2}House Peake
Faction.text: House Peake is an ancient noble house of the Reach whose lands lie along the western marches of the region. Once among the most powerful houses of the Reach, the Peakes historically held three castles: Starpike, Dunstonbury, and Whitegrove, and their influence rivaled that of many greater lords. Their sigil bears three black castles on an orange field. House words: 'Our Roots Go Deep'. Despite their ancient standing, the power of House Peake has long been marked by ambition and conflict, and members of the house played a prominent role in the First Blackfyre Rebellion, declaring for Daemon Blackfyre against King Daeron II Targaryen. By the time of the rebellion, House Peake remains a formidable marcher house of the Reach, commanding strong castles and a proud lineage that traces back to the days before Aegon's Conquest.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_3}House Tarly of Horn Hill
Faction.text: House Tarly rules from Horn Hill, a strong castle in the northern Reach overlooking the fertile lands and forests that lie along the upper Mander. The Tarlys are known throughout the realm for their martial tradition and disciplined soldiers, and their lords have long served as some of the most capable battlefield commanders among the bannermen of House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their sigil bears a red huntsman on a green field, and their ancestral greatsword Heartsbane, forged of Valyrian steel, has passed through the line of Tarly lords for generations. House words: 'First in Battle'. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Tarly stands as one of the foremost military houses of the Reach, sworn to Highgarden and ready to answer the call when the banners of the Reach are raised.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_4}House Ashford
Faction.text: House Ashford is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, ruling from the castle of Ashford along the banks of the Cockleswhent. Their lands lie in the fertile heart of the Reach where fields, orchards, and market roads connect the great castles and towns of the region. The house bears a white sun in splendor on an orange field. Though not counted among the greatest houses of the Reach, House Ashford holds an established place among the Tyrell bannermen and commands respectable lands and knights in service to Highgarden. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Ashford remains a loyal house of the Reach, supporting the rule of House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_5}House Fossoway of Ciderhall
Faction.text: House Fossoway of Cider Hall is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, ruling from their seat in the fertile orchard lands along the Mander. The Fossoways are known for the rich apple harvests of their lands, and their sigil bears a red apple on a silver field. House words: 'Taste of Glory'. During the First Blackfyre Rebellion, the house divided when Ser Derrick Fossoway declared for Daemon Blackfyre, founding the Green Apple Fossoways after taking a green apple as his sigil, while the main line of House Fossoway remained loyal to King Daeron II Targaryen. By the time of the rebellion, the Red Apple Fossoways of Cider Hall continue to serve among the bannermen of Highgarden, holding their lands along the Mander and maintaining their place among the established noble houses of the Reach.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_6}House Merryweather of Longtable
Faction.text: House Merryweather rules from Longtable, a castle in the Reach situated along the Mander, where their lands benefit from the fertile soil and river trade that define the region. As bannermen sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, the Merryweathers hold a respected place among the landed nobility of the Reach. Their sigil bears a horn of plenty spilling fruit and grain on a blue field. Though not among the greatest houses of the region, House Merryweather commands productive lands and contributes knights and soldiers to the armies of Highgarden when the banners of the Reach are called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Merryweather remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Longtable and its surrounding lands in service to House Tyrell and the rule of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_7}House Meadows of Grassyvale
Faction.text: House Meadows rules from Grassy Vale, a seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their lands lie within the fertile countryside that defines the region, contributing to the wealth and abundance of the Reach through agriculture and local levies. The house bears a green field charged with a white flower. Though not among the most powerful bannermen of Highgarden, House Meadows holds an established place among the landed nobility of the Reach and provides service when the banners are called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Meadows remains a loyal house of the Reach, maintaining its lands and fulfilling its obligations to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_8}House Caswell of Bitterbridge
Faction.text: House Caswell rules from Bitterbridge, a vital crossing over the Mander in the Reach, where roads and river traffic converge, making it one of the most strategically important locations in the region. As bannermen sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, the Caswells control trade and movement through this central artery of the Reach. Their sigil bears a centaur drawing a bow on a black field. Though not among the greatest houses of the Reach, House Caswell holds considerable regional importance due to its control of Bitterbridge and the surrounding lands. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Caswell remains a loyal house of the Reach, guarding the crossing of the Mander and supporting the rule of House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_9}House Footly of Tumbleton
Faction.text: House Footly rules from Tumbleton, a market town in the Reach situated along the Mander, where fertile lands and river trade support its prosperity. As bannermen sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, the Footlys hold a modest but important position among the landed nobility of the region, overseeing a key settlement that serves as a center of commerce and travel. Their sigil bears a winged chalice on a red field. Though not among the most powerful houses of the Reach, House Footly benefits from the wealth of Tumbleton and provides men and service to Highgarden when called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Footly remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Tumbleton and its surrounding lands under the authority of House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_10}House Ball
Faction.text: House Ball is a noble house of the Crownlands sworn to House Targaryen, with lands situated near the heart of the realm. The house bears three red roundels on a gold field. Though not among the great houses of the realm, House Ball is remembered for its martial tradition, most notably through Ser Quentyn Ball, called Fireball, a renowned knight and master-at-arms who played a significant role in training Daemon Blackfyre.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_11}House Risley of Risley Glade
Faction.text: House Risley is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, ruling from Risley Glade, a holding in the eastern Reach near the borders of the Crownlands. Their lands lie within fertile countryside that supports the wealth and abundance of the region while also placing them along routes that connect the Reach to the heart of the realm. According to semi-canon sources, their arms depict a knight mounted on a rearing horse on a field, bearing a golden lance and a white shield. Though not among the most powerful bannermen of Highgarden, House Risley holds an established place among the landed nobility of the Reach and provides men and service when the banners are called. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Risley remains a loyal house of the Reach, maintaining Risley Glade and its surrounding lands under the authority of House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_12}House Vyrwell of Darkdell
Faction.text: House Vyrwel rules from Darkdell, a castle in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their lands lie in the western Reach, and they stand among the established bannermen who support the authority of Highgarden across the region. Their arms bear a silver wyvern within a red double tressure on a sable field. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen, House Vyrwel remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Darkdell and its surrounding lands while providing service and levies to House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_13}House Osgrey of Standfast
Faction.text: House Osgrey of Standfast is a knightly branch of House Osgrey, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, holding a small tower and modest lands in the Reach. Once part of a far more powerful house that ruled from Coldmoat, the Osgreys have declined over generations, losing much of their former strength and influence. Their sigil bears a chequy lion, and their lineage remains proud despite their reduced circumstances.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_14}House Webber of Coldmoat
Faction.text: House Webber rules from Coldmoat, a castle in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their lands lie along the borderlands of the Reach where rivalries with neighboring houses, particularly House Osgrey, have shaped their recent history. Their arms bear a black spider on a silver field.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_15}House Rowan of Goldengrove
Faction.text: House Rowan rules from Goldengrove, a great castle in the northern Reach surrounded by fertile lands and broad fields that contribute to the wealth of the region. As one of the most powerful bannermen sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, the Rowans command significant strength and influence within the Reach. Their arms bear a golden tree on a silver field.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_16}House Appleton
Faction.text: House Appleton rules from Appleton, a small seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, situated along the Mander amid fertile orchard lands. The house is modest in strength but benefits from the rich agricultural output of the region and the trade that flows along the river. Their arms bear a red apple on a yellow field.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_17}House Graceford of Holyhall
Faction.text: House Graceford rules from Holyhall, a castle in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands situated near the great roads that connect the Reach to the Crownlands. As an established noble house of the region, the Gracefords hold fertile lands and contribute men and service to Highgarden when called. Their arms bear the Mother’s face upon a white pale wavy dividing a brown field. House Graceford remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Holyhall and its surrounding lands while supporting the rule of House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_18}House Crane of Red Lake
Faction.text: House Crane rules from Red Lake, a castle in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands centered around the lake of the same name in the western Reach. As one of the notable houses of the region, the Cranes command fertile lands and maintain a respected position among the bannermen of Highgarden. Their arms bear a vee of golden cranes on a pale blue field. House Crane remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Red Lake and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_19}House Oakheart of Old Oak
Faction.text: House Oakheart rules from Old Oak, a strong castle in the western Reach overlooking the Sunset Sea, where their lands guard the coastal approaches and fertile countryside of the region. As one of the more prominent bannermen sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, the Oakhearts command considerable strength and influence within the Reach. Their arms bear a green oak tree on a golden field. House Oakheart remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Old Oak and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_20}House Shermer of Smithyton
Faction.text: House Shermer rules from Smithyton their seat located in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, situated where trade and fertile lands support its prosperity. Though not among the most powerful houses of the region, the Shermers hold a modest but important position overseeing a center of commerce and craft within the Reach. Their arms bear a burning forge on a black field. House Shermer remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Smithyton and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne. House Shermer blazons House Shermer blazons its shields with a field of copper nails on blue, a copper border rayonne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_21}House Cordwayner of Hammerhal
Faction.text: House Cordwayner rules from Hammerhal, a seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Though little is recorded of their lands or history, they stand among the lesser nobility that support Tyrell authority across the region. Their arms bear black boots on a field of green and gold diamonds. House Cordwayner remains a minor but established house of the Reach, holding Hammerhal and its surrounding lands while providing service and levies to Highgarden and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_22}House Hewett of Oakenshield
Faction.text: House Hewett rules from Oakenshield, the easternmost of the Shield Islands, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their seat is Lord Hewett's Town, a large port on the island, above whose harbor stands Lord Hewett's castle, a small but strong fortress with thick walls and studded oaken gates. Their arms bear an oaken escutcheon studded with iron on a field bendy of undulating blue and white. House Hewett holds an important place in the defense of the Reach, for the Shield Islands stand as the first line of protection at the mouth of the Mander, guarding against the ironborn pirates who once sought to sail upriver and sack the wealth of the Reach. House Hewett remains a loyal house of the Reach, providing ships and men in service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_23}House Chester of Greenshield
Faction.text: House Chester rules from Greenshield, one of the Shield Islands off the western coast of the Reach, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their lands lie upon the island they govern, and they share in the duty of defending the mouth of the Mander from raiders of the Sunset Sea. Their arms bear a green hand on a gold escutcheon on a green field, with a border of red rayonne. House Chester stands among the island lords tasked with guarding the western approaches to the Reach, maintaining ships and men to repel incursions and protect the fertile heartlands beyond.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_24}House Grimm of Greyshield
Faction.text: House Grimm rules from Greyshield, one of the Shield Islands off the western coast of the Reach, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their seat includes Grimston, a settlement upon the island, and their lands lie along the western approaches where they share in the duty of defending the mouth of the Mander from raiders of the Sunset Sea. Their arms bear an iron escutcheon with silver studs on a grey-green field strewn with longships proper. House Grimm stands among the island lords tasked with guarding the western approaches to the Reach, maintaining ships and men to protect the fertile heartlands beyond.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_25}House Serry of Southshield
Faction.text: House Serry rules from Southshield, one of the Shield Islands off the western coast of the Reach, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their lands lie upon the island they govern, and like the other Shield Island lords they share in the duty of defending the mouth of the Mander from raiders of the Sunset Sea. Their arms bear a white rose upon a red escutcheon upon white, within a red embattled border. House Serry stands among the island lords tasked with guarding the western approaches to the Reach, maintaining ships and men to protect the fertile heartlands beyond.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_26}House Florent of Brightwater Keep
Faction.text: House Florent rules from Brightwater Keep, a strong castle in the Reach near the mouth of the Honeywine, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. As one of the more prominent houses of the Reach, the Florents command fertile lands and maintain notable influence among the Tyrell bannermen, often asserting an ancient claim of descent from Garth Greenhand. Their arms bear a fox’s head within a blue ring on a white field. House Florent stands among the established noble houses of the Reach, holding Brightwater Keep and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_27}House Blackbar of Bandallon
Faction.text: House Blackbar rules from Bandallon, a coastal seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with lands overlooking the Sunset Sea. Their position along the western shores places them among the houses responsible for guarding the coast and supporting the defenses of the region. Their arms bear a black fess on silver. House Blackbar stands among the established noble houses of the Reach, holding Bandallon and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_28}House Bulwer of Blackcrown
Faction.text: House Bulwer rules from Blackcrown, a castle in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands lying near the western coasts and fertile countryside of the region. House Bulwer is an established noble house among the Tyrell bannermen, contributing men and strength to the Reach when called. Their arms bear a bull’s skull, bone over blood. House Bulwer remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Blackcrown and its surrounding lands while providing service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_29}House Hightower of the Hightower
Faction.text: House Hightower rules from the Hightower at Oldtown, one of the oldest and largest cities in the Reach, sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. The Hightower itself rises above the harbor at the mouth of the Honeywine, serving as both fortress and beacon to ships entering the city. Oldtown is a center of trade, learning, and faith, home to the Citadel and long associated with the Starry Sept, giving House Hightower immense wealth and influence within the realm. As one of the most powerful bannermen of the Reach, they command vast resources and maintain close ties to both the Faith and the maesters. Their arms bear a white tower crowned with fire on a grey field. House Hightower stands among the foremost noble houses of the Seven Kingdoms, holding the Hightower and its surrounding lands while providing strength and support to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_30}House Beesbury of Honeyholt
Faction.text: House Beesbury rules from Honeyholt, a seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands situated along the Honeywine where fertile fields and orchards support their holdings. The house is closely associated with beekeeping and the production of honey, which contributes to their local wealth and identity within the region. Their arms bear three beehives. House Beesbury remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Honeyholt and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_31}House Mullendore of Uplands
Faction.text: House Mullendore rules from Uplands, a seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands situated within the fertile countryside of the region. House Mullendore is an established noble house among the Tyrell bannermen, contributing men and strength to the Reach when called. Their arms depict a field of black-and-orange butterflies strewn over white. House Mullendore remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Uplands and its surrounding lands while providing service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_32}House Ambrose
Faction.text: House Ambrose is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands situated in the fertile countryside of the region. They stand among the landed nobility that support Tyrell authority and contribute men and service when called. Their arms bear a yellow field strewn with red ants. House words: 'Never Resting'. House Ambrose remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding their lands while providing strength and support to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_33}House Cockshaw
Faction.text: House Cockshaw is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands situated within the fertile countryside of the region. They stand among the lesser nobility that support Tyrell authority and provide men and service when called. Their arms bear three feathers, red, white, and gold, on a black field. House Cockshaw remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding their lands while contributing to the strength and stability of the Reach under the authority of House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_34}House Costayne of Three Towers
Faction.text: House Costayne rules from Three Towers, a coastal seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands overlooking the Sunset Sea. Their position along the western shores places them among the houses responsible for guarding the coast and supporting the defenses of the region. Their arms bear quartered: a silver chalice on black and a black rose on yellow. House Costayne stands among the established noble houses of the Reach, holding Three Towers and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_35}House Cuy of Sunhouse
Faction.text: House Cuy rules from Sunhouse, a seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden. Their lands include the town of Cuy, and they stand among the lesser nobility that support Tyrell authority and provide men and service when called. Their arms bear six yellow flowers on a blue field. House Cuy remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding Sunhouse and its surrounding lands while contributing to the strength and stability of the Reach under the authority of House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_36}House Redwyne of the Arbor
Faction.text: House Redwyne rules from the Arbor, a rich island domain in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, renowned for its vineyards and the production of some of the finest wines in the Seven Kingdoms. Their seat is Ryamsport, a great harbor and stronghold from which they command one of the most powerful fleets in the realm, giving them significant influence both at sea and within the politics of the Reach. Their arms bear a burgundy grape cluster on a blue field. House Redwyne stands among the foremost noble houses of the Reach, holding the Arbor and its lands while providing ships, wealth, and strength to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_37}House Roxton of the Ring
Faction.text: House Roxton rules from the Ring, a seat in the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands situated in the fertile countryside of the region. House Roxton is an established noble house among the Tyrell bannermen, contributing men and strength to the Reach when called. Their arms bear a saltire of interlocked golden rings upon a sky blue field. They possess the Valyrian steel sword Orphan-Maker, a weapon of great renown within the Reach. House Roxton remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding the Ring and its surrounding lands while providing service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_38}House Norcross
Faction.text: House Norcross is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, with their lands situated within the fertile countryside of the region. They stand among the lesser nobility that support Tyrell authority and provide men and service when called. Their arms bear a black saltire on a white field. House Norcross remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding their lands while contributing to the strength and stability of the Reach under the authority of House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_39}House Rhysling
Faction.text: House Rhysling is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, ruling as marcher lords in the highlands of the Red Mountains along the border between the Reach and Dorne. Their lands lie in a rugged and defensive region where watchfulness and martial strength are required to guard the passes and maintain the peace along the frontier. Their arms bear an oaken door banded with iron, set within a grey stone doorway, on black masonry. House Rhysling stands among the border lords tasked with securing the southern marches, holding their lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_40}House Hunt
Faction.text: House Hunt is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tarly of Horn Hill, serving as marcher lords along the fringes of the region where vigilance and martial strength are required. Their lands lie within the Reach near the borderlands, and they stand among the lesser nobility that support Tarly authority and provide men and service when called. Their arms bear a brown deer, bound and slung on a pole, on a white field. House Hunt remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding their lands while contributing to the defense of the marches and the authority of House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_41}House Redding
Faction.text: House Redding is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, counted among the landed nobility that support Tyrell authority across the region. Their lands lie within the Reach, and they provide men and service when the banners are called. Their arms bear a golden flagon on a burgundy field within a border of gold and white checks. House Redding remains a loyal house of the Reach, holding their lands while contributing to the strength and stability of the realm under the authority of House Tyrell and the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_REACH_42}House Bridges
Faction.text: House Bridges is a noble house of the Reach sworn to House Tyrell of Highgarden, ruling from a seat south of the Cockleswhent crossing near Ashford, where they serve as marcher lords guarding the approaches into the heartland of the Reach. Their position along this vital route places them in charge of securing movement across the river and maintaining order along the borderlands. Their arms bear a black stone bridge with three arches upon a golden chief above three blue pallets on a white field. House Bridges stands among the border lords tasked with defending the crossings of the Reach, holding their lands while providing men and service to House Tyrell and the authority of the Iron Throne

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_1}House Martell
Faction.text: House Martell rules from Sunspear, the seat of power in Dorne, where the ruling princes govern the southernmost realm of the Seven Kingdoms. Unlike the other great houses, the Martells hold the title of Prince and Princess, reflecting Dorne’s unique status and its unbroken traditions following its peaceful union with the Iron Throne under King Daeron II Targaryen. Their lands span deserts, river valleys, and the Red Mountains, and their rule is shaped by Rhoynar customs brought by Nymeria, including equal inheritance regardless of gender. Their arms bear a red sun pierced by a golden spear on an orange field. House Martell stands as the dominant power of Dorne, holding Sunspear and its lands while maintaining their authority as Princes of Dorne under the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_2}House Santagar of Spottswood
Faction.text: House Santagar rules from Spottswood, a seat in Dorne sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, with their lands lying along the coastal regions of the principality. As an established noble house of Dorne, the Santagars stand among the bannermen who support Martell authority and uphold the customs of the region. Their arms bear a spotted leopard with a golden axe over a per bend sinister blue and white field. House Santagar remains a loyal house of Dorne, holding Spottswood and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Martell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_3}House Toland of Ghost Hill
Faction.text: House Toland rules from Ghost Hill, a seat in Dorne sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, standing among the principal bannermen of the Dornish princes. Their lands lie along the eastern coast of Dorne, where they have long held their position through resilience and cunning. Their arms bear a green dragon biting its tail on a gold field, a symbol adopted after the First Dornish War, replacing their earlier banner of a ghost. House Toland remains a loyal house of Dorne, holding Ghost Hill and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Martell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_4}House Dalt of Lemonwood
Faction.text: House Dalt rules from Lemonwood, a seat in Dorne sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, located south of the mouth of the Greenblood near the coast. As a knightly house, their head bears the title Knight of Lemonwood, and they stand among the lesser but established nobility that support Martell authority across the principality. Their arms bear a purple field strewn with lemons. House Dalt remains a loyal house of Dorne, holding Lemonwood and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Martell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_5}House Allyrion of Godsgrace
Faction.text: House Dalt rules from Lemonwood, a seat in Dorne sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, located south of the mouth of the Greenblood near the coast. As a knightly house, their head bears the title Knight of Lemonwood, and they stand among the lesser but established nobility that support Martell authority across the principality. Their arms bear a purple field strewn with lemons. House Dalt remains a loyal house of Dorne, holding Lemonwood and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Martell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_6}House Gargalen of Salt Shore
Faction.text: House Gargalen rules from Salt Shore, a coastal seat in Dorne sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, with their lands lying along the southern shores where the deserts meet the sea. As an established noble house of Dorne, the Gargalens stand among the Martell bannermen who uphold the authority of Sunspear across the principality. Their arms bear a red cockatrice on a sandy field. House Gargalen remains a loyal house of Dorne, holding Salt Shore and its surrounding lands while providing men and service to House Martell and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_7}House Jordayne of the Tor
Faction.text: House Jordayne of the Tor is a noble house of Dorne, seated at the Tor, and they bear a golden quill on checkered dark and light green with the words 'Let It Be Written.' They are sworn to House Martell of Sunspear. The house traces its origins to the coming of the Andals in Dorne and once ruled as kings at the Tor, with even the Martells bending the knee to them in earlier days. During Nymeria’s War, they stood among the bannermen of King Yorick Yronwood against Mors Martell and his Rhoynar. In the First Dornish War, the Tor was taken by Aegon I Targaryen after the death of a Jordayne lord, only to be retaken by the Dornish, and later burned by Visenya Targaryen upon Vhagar. In later wars, the Tor served as a mustering point for Dornish forces, including during the campaign of Prince Morion Martell.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_8}House Ladybright
Faction.text: House Ladybright is a noble house of Dorne, sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, and counted among the lesser Dornish houses that support the rule of the Martells and answer their call in times of war.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_9}House Drinkwater
Faction.text: House Drinkwater is a noble house of Dorne, sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, and counted among the Dornish houses that support the rule of the Martells and answer their call in times of war.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_10}House Wells
Faction.text: House Wells is a noble house of Dorne, sworn to House Martell of Sunspear, and counted among the Dornish houses that support the rule of the Martells and answer their call in times of war.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_11}House Vaith of the Red Dunes
Faction.text: House Vaith of the Red Dunes is a noble house of Dorne, seated at Vaith upon the river of the same name, and they are sworn to House Martell of Sunspear. Their origins trace back to Andal adventurers who settled the region and raised their seat where the waters meet the sands, taking the title Lords of the Red Dunes. During the wars of Aegon I Targaryen, the castle of Vaith was found abandoned by Rhaenys Targaryen, later contested in the First Dornish War when forces sent against it vanished in the sands, and the lord of the house was slain during the Dragon’s Wroth for the bounty placed upon him. In the time of Daeron I Targaryen, the Vaiths bent the knee during the submission of Dorne, and in later years Lady Cassella Vaith was taken as a mistress by Aegon IV Targaryen, a mark of the house's presence among the notable blood of Dorne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_12}House Yronwood
Faction.text: House Yronwood of Yronwood is an ancient house of First Men origin, seated in the foothills of the Red Mountains where they command the Stone Way and guard one of the chief passes into Dorne. Their blazon is a black portcullis grill over sand, and their words are 'We Guard the Way'. In ages past the Yronwood kings held broad dominion in northern Dorne and claimed the style of High King of Dorne, oft contending with the kings of House Fowler, House Dayne, and the storm kings beyond the mountains. In Nymeria’s War, King Yorick V Yronwood withstood Nymeria and Mors Martell for many years, and slew Mors in the Third Battle of the Boneway before at last submitting, after which the Yronwoods became the mightiest bannermen of House Martell. By 196 AC they remain the most powerful house in Dorne after Sunspear, and their lord still bears the ancient title of the Bloodroyal.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_13}House Uller of Hellholt
Faction.text: House Uller of Hellholt is a great Dornish house seated at Hellholt along the Brimstone in the deep desert, founded by Andal adventurers who settled those harsh lands. Their arms are rayonne yellow over crimson, said to be inspired by the burning of rival lords who were invited into Hellholt, locked within, and put to death by fire, from which the castle takes its name. During Nymeria’s War the Ullers supported Nymeria and House Martell against House Yronwood, and Nymeria took a Lord Uller as her second husband after the death of Mors Martell. At the outset of the First Dornish War, the Ullers abandoned Hellholt rather than face the invading host of Lord Harlan Tyrell, and later in the conflict Queen Rhaenys Targaryen and her dragon Meraxes died at Hellholt in 10 AC, though her body was not recovered. During the Dragon’s Wroth, four successive Lords of Hellholt were slain for the bounties placed upon them by the Iron Throne, and House Uller remains sworn to House Martell of Sunspear.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_14}House Qorgyle of Sandstone
Faction.text: House Qorgyle of Sandstone is a Dornish house seated at Sandstone in the deep deserts, where their forebears, adventuring Andals, settled and fortified the only well for many leagues, raising their castle amidst the sands. Their arms are three black scorpions on red, and no house words are recorded. By the time of Nymeria’s War, the Qorgyles were bannermen to King Yorick V Yronwood and supported him against Nymeria and House Martell, but after his defeat they bent the knee and became vassals of Sunspear. In the First Dornish War they did not submit to Aegon I Targaryen, and their spearmen harried the forces of Lord Harlan Tyrell, while Sandstone itself was later burned in 9 AC by the dragons of Aegon and Queen Visenya Targaryen. During the conquest of Dorne under King Daeron I Targaryen, Lord Qorgyle aided in suppressing rebellion, though Lord Lyonel Tyrell was later found dead at Sandstone in a bed of scorpions, an act attributed by some to Qorgyle design, though never proven.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_15}House Dayne of Starfall
Faction.text: House Dayne of Starfall is one of the most ancient houses in the Seven Kingdoms, seated at Starfall upon an island at the mouth of the Torrentine where it meets the Summer Sea. Their blazon is a white sword and falling star crossed on a lilac field. The Daynes trace their origin to the dawn of days, when their first forebear followed a falling star to the site of Starfall and raised his seat there, from which their line became Kings of the Torrentine and Lords of Starfall. Counted among the most powerful houses of ancient Dorne alongside Houses Fowler and Yronwood, they contended often with the Reach, and King Samwell Dayne, called Starfire, once burned Oldtown. In Nymeria’s War, King Vorian Dayne, known as the Sword of the Evening, was defeated and sent to the Wall, after which the Daynes supported Nymeria, whose third husband was Ser Davos Dayne, the Sword of the Morning. The house is famed for Dawn, their ancestral blade forged from the fallen star, borne only by those judged worthy to be named Sword of the Morning. During the First Dornish War, Ser Joffrey Dayne led forces against Oldtown, and in turn Starfall was burned by Queen Visenya Targaryen. House Dayne remains among the principal houses sworn to Sunspear, their ancient honors and traditions enduring.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_16}House Dayne of High Hermitage
Faction.text: House Dayne of High Hermitage is a cadet branch of House Dayne of Starfall, seated at High Hermitage in Dorne. Their arms are a black sword and falling star on white, the inverse of their parent house. They descend from the ancient line of the Daynes and share in that house’s First Men heritage.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_17}House Fowler of Skyreach
Faction.text: House Fowler of Skyreach is an ancient Dornish house of First Men origin, seated at Skyreach in the Prince’s Pass where they guard the Wide Way between Dorne and the Reach. Their blazon is a hooded blue hawk on silver, and their words are 'Let Me Soar'. In ancient days they were kings, styling themselves Kings of Stone and Sky and Lords of the Wide Way, counted among the greatest powers of Dorne alongside Houses Dayne and Yronwood, and oft warred with the marcher lords of the Reach, as when King Ferris Fowler led ten thousand men north but was turned back by King Garth VII Gardener. During Nymeria’s War, after the fall of King Garrison Fowler who was sent to the Wall, the Fowlers sided with Nymeria and House Martell against House Yronwood, beginning a rivalry that endures thereafter. In the First Dornish War, Lord Fowler led a host that burned Nightsong and took captives, though Skyreach later suffered in the Dragon’s Wroth and a Lord Fowler was slain for the bounty placed upon him by the Iron Throne. By 196 AC, House Fowler remains one of the principal houses of Dorne, holding the Prince’s Pass in service to Sunspear and maintaining their ancient enmity with House Yronwood.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_18}House Blackmont
Faction.text: House Blackmont of Blackmont is a Dornish house seated in the Red Mountains, counted among the stony Dornish who dwell along the marches. Their blazon is a black vulture bearing a pink infant in its claws on yellow. In the days before Nymeria’s coming, the Blackmonts were kings in their own right, yet in that war King Benedict Blackmont was defeated and sent to the Wall among the six kings taken by Nymeria, having cast his strength behind House Yronwood against Nymeria and House Martell. Thereafter the house bent the knee and entered the service of Sunspear. The Blackmonts have long contested the marcher lords beyond the mountains, and by the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen they remain a noble house of Dorne, their lands set against the passes and their loyalties sworn to House Martell.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_19}House Manwoody of Kingsgrave
Faction.text: House Manwoody of Kingsgrave is a Dornish house seated at Kingsgrave in the Prince’s Pass, where it guards one of the chief approaches into Dorne through the Red Mountains. Their blazon is a white skull crowned with gold on black. In ancient days they were kings, and King Albin Manwoody claimed dominion over the Red Mountains until he was defeated during Nymeria’s War by Nymeria and Mors Martell, after which he was sent to the Wall in golden fetters alongside other fallen kings. The name of Kingsgrave and the crowned skull upon their banners are said to derive from the slaying of a king in those lands. The Manwoodys have long skirmished with the marcher lords beyond the mountains, holding their position along the pass.

Faction.name: {=LOC_DORNE_20}House Wyl of the Boneway
Faction.text: House Wyl of the Boneway is a Dornish house seated at Wyl in the northern Red Mountains, where they hold the Boneway, one of the chief passes into Dorne. Their blazon is a black adder biting a heel on yellow. The Wyls have long warred against the marcher lords to the north and are known for their cunning defenses, having tunneled beneath their castle to link caverns for greater protection. In ancient days they fought beside the Yronwood kings, as seen when Wylla of Wyl rode with Yoren Yronwood against King Durran at the Battle by the Bloody Pool. During the First Dornish War, the Wyl of Wyl ambushed and defeated the forces of Lord Orys Baratheon in the Boneway, later ransoming him and his men before maiming them upon release, an act that brought lasting vengeance upon his line. The house also committed notorious atrocities during that war, and in later years played a role in the death of King Daeron I Targaryen under a peace banner, whilst also holding Prince Aemon the Dragonknight captive until his release was secured.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_1}House Stark of Winterfell
Faction.text: House Stark of Winterfell is one of the most ancient and powerful houses of the North, seated at Winterfell, whose hot springs keep its walls warm even in the depths of winter. Their blazon is a grey direwolf on white, and their words are 'Winter Is Coming'. The Starks trace their lineage to the First Men and to Bran the Builder, who is said to have raised Winterfell and aided in the building of the Wall. In ancient days they were Kings in the North, ruling for thousands of years, subduing rival kings such as the Barrow Kings and the Marsh Kings, and taking the Wolfswood and White Knife under their dominion. Torrhen Stark, the last King in the North, bent the knee to Aegon I Targaryen rather than see his people destroyed by dragons, after which House Stark became Wardens of the North under the Iron Throne. By the reign of King Daeron II Targaryen in 196 AC, the Starks remain the paramount house of the North, ruling from Winterfell and maintaining the ancient traditions of the First Men.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_2}House Cerwyn
Faction.text: House Cerwyn rules from Castle Cerwyn, a seat in the North sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, located south of Winterfell within the central lands of the region. As one of the closest bannermen to Winterfell, the Cerwyns have long held a loyal and dependable position among the northern nobility. Lord Cerwyn was believed to have been the closest friend of Cregan Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, and supported the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons, later being present at court in King’s Landing during the Hour of the Wolf. Their arms bear a black battle axe on a silver field. House words: 'Honed and Ready'. House Cerwyn remains a steadfast house of the North, holding Castle Cerwyn and its surrounding lands while supporting the rule of House Stark and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_3}House Condon
Faction.text: House Cerwyn rules from Castle Cerwyn, a seat in the North sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, located south of Winterfell within the central lands of the region. As one of the closest bannermen to Winterfell, the Cerwyns have long held a loyal and dependable position among the northern nobility. Lord Cerwyn was believed to have been the closest friend of Cregan Stark, Lord of Winterfell and Warden of the North, and supported the blacks during the Dance of the Dragons, later being present at court in King’s Landing during the Hour of the Wolf. Their arms bear a black battle axe on a silver field. House words: 'Honed and Ready'. House Cerwyn remains a steadfast house of the North, holding Castle Cerwyn and its surrounding lands while supporting the rule of House Stark and the authority of the Iron Throne.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_4}Clan Flint of the Moutains
Faction.text: House Flint of the Mountains also known as Flint of Breakstone Hill is a northern house of First Men descent, seated at Breakstone Hill and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, with their chief known simply as 'The Flint'. They are regarded as one of several branches of House Flint, distinct from those of Flint's Finger and Widow's Watch, with the mountain clansmen naming themselves the First Flints and holding that the other branches were founded by younger sons who departed the hills in search of land and wives. It is held that these Flints of the mountains may be the same line as the ancient kings who ruled after the Long Night before being brought to heel by the Kings of Winter. In later ages, the Flints are tied to several notable accounts, including Lord Commander Rodrik Flint of the Night's Watch, remembered for attempting to crown himself King-Beyond-the-Wall, and the grim tale of Danny Flint at the Nightfort. A branch of the house held the Wolf's Den for a century following the fall of House Greystark, and the Flints of the hills are recorded to have aided Lord Cregan Stark in the suppression of free folk raiders led by Sylas the Grim, demonstrating their continued loyalty to Winterfell across the centuries.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_5}Clan Liddle
Faction.text: House Liddle is a mountain clan of the North, descended from the First Men and dwelling in the northern hills, where they live apart from the feudal order of the great houses yet remain loyal to House Stark of Winterfell. Their chief is styled 'the Liddle', in accordance with the customs of the clans. Their arms are described as per pale white and green, a fir tree line between, with three brown pinecones upon the white. The Liddles are counted among those clans who keep the old ways, living in harsh and remote lands, and are known to answer the call of Winterfell in times of need, bound by kinship, tradition, and long-standing allegiance to the Kings of Winter.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_6}Clan Wull
Faction.text: House Wull is a mountain clan of the North, descended from the First Men and dwelling in the hills, where they remain outside the feudal customs of the lowlands yet are sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. Their chief is known as 'the Wull', in keeping with the traditions of the northern clans. They blazon their banners with three wooden buckets, brown upon blue, bordered by grey and white checks. The Wulls are known for their fierce temper and proud independence, and they bear a long-standing hatred for the ironborn, stemming from repeated raids along the Bay of Ice in earlier centuries. Like the other hill clans, they keep to the old ways and answer the call of Winterfell when summoned, fighting with a reputation for ferocity and endurance.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_7}Clan Norrey
Faction.text: House Norrey is a mountain clan of the North, descended from the First Men and dwelling in the hills, where they remain bound to the old ways and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. Their chief is known as 'the Norrey', following the customs of the clans. They blazon their banners with six green thistles upon a yellow field. The Norreys have long been regarded as friendly toward the Night's Watch, and it is counted among the many suggestions that the Night's King may have been of their line. In the age of Lord Cregan Stark, Lady Arra Norrey was taken as his first wife, and the Norreys are recorded to have aided him in the hunting of the free folk raiders led by Sylas the Grim, further affirming their loyalty to Winterfell and their place among the steadfast clans of the northern mountains.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_8}Clan Burley
Faction.text: House Norrey is a mountain clan of the North, descended from the First Men and dwelling in the hills, where they remain bound to the old ways and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. Their chief is known as 'the Norrey', following the customs of the clans. They blazon their banners with six green thistles upon a yellow field. The Norreys have long been regarded as friendly toward the Night's Watch, and it is counted among the many suggestions that the Night's King may have been of their line. In the age of Lord Cregan Stark, Lady Arra Norrey was taken as his first wife, and the Norreys are recorded to have aided him in the hunting of the free folk raiders led by Sylas the Grim, further affirming their loyalty to Winterfell and their place among the steadfast clans of the northern mountains.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_9}Clan Knott
Faction.text: House Knott is a mountain clan of the North, descended from the First Men and dwelling in the northern hills, where they live according to the customs of the clans while remaining sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. Their chief is known as 'the Knott', in keeping with the traditions of the mountain clans. The Knotts are counted among those who keep the old ways, living in harsh and isolated lands, and are bound by kinship and ancient loyalty, answering the call of Winterfell in times of need.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_10}Clan Harclay
Faction.text: House Harclay is a mountain clan of the North, descended from the First Men and dwelling in the hills north of the Wolfswood, where they live according to the customs of the clans while remaining sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. Their chief is known as 'the Harclay', in keeping with the traditions of the northern clans. They blazon their shield with three blue moons, waxing, full, and waning, set upon a white bend on a blue field. Like the other hill clans, the Harclays are bound by kinship and ancient tradition, holding to the old ways and answering the call of Winterfell in times of need.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_11}House Whitehill of Highpoint
Faction.text: House Whitehill of Highpoint is a northern house, with their seat at Highpoint, located in the hills between the northern Wolfswood and the northern mountains, north of Winterfell. They are said to have once possessed a great ironwood forest, though their holdings have since been diminished through mismanagement. They blazon their arms with a white pile inverted on an indigo field, with an arch of four-pointed stars above. The Whitehills are counted among the landed nobility of the region, their fortunes tied to the feudal order of the North, and they are known to hold a bitter rivalry with House Forrester.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_12}House Umber of Last Hearth
Faction.text: House Umber of the Last Hearth is an ancient noble house of the North, seated at the Last Hearth and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. Their sigil is a roaring giant, brown-haired and clad in skins, with broken silver chains upon a flame-red field. The Umbers are known for their great size and boisterous nature, often bearing thick beards and long hair. Their lands stretch along the Bay of Seals, south of the New Gift and west to the kingsroad, encompassing the northern Last River, the Lonely Hills, and forests of pine and oak, with herds grazed in the high mountain meadows during summer. Once kings in the age of the First Men, the Umbers were brought to heel by the Kings of Winter and became among House Stark’s most powerful bannermen. They joined the Starks in the defeat of Gendel and Gorne, and in later centuries stood against King Raymun Redbeard at Long Lake, where Lord Harmond Umber fought beside the Starks. Queen Alysanne Targaryen is recorded to have visited the Last Hearth in 58 AC, and among the tales of the North it is said that the Night’s King may have been of Umber blood.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_13}House Mollen
Faction.text: House Mollen is a noble house of the North, sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a pine tree covered with snow in a pale green pile, on white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_14}House Karstark of Karhold
Faction.text: House Karstark of Karhold is a noble house of the North, seated at Karhold and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, from whom they descend. Their line traces back to Karlon Stark, a younger son of Winterfell, who was granted lands after putting down a rebel lord in ancient days, raising a castle that came to be known as Karhold, and from whom the name Karstark is derived. They bear for arms a white sunburst upon a black field, and their words are 'The Sun of Winter'. Counted among the principal bannermen of Winterfell, the Karstarks have long maintained close ties with their Stark kin, with marriages recorded between the two houses across the centuries, and they are known in the North as fierce and steadfast, keeping to the customs and martial traditions of the First Men.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_15}House Bolton of the Dreadfort
Faction.text: House Bolton of the Dreadfort is an ancient noble house of the North, seated at the Dreadfort upon the Weeping Water and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a flayed man, red upon pink, and their words are 'Our Blades Are Sharp'. The Boltons trace their lineage to the First Men and once ruled as kings in the eastern North before being subdued by the Kings of Winter, after which they became among the most powerful of Stark bannermen. Their history is marked by frequent rebellion against Winterfell in earlier ages, and they are long associated with the ancient and forbidden practice of flaying, a custom said to have been abandoned after their submission, though the memory of it endures in their sigil and sinister reputation.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_16}House Hornwood
Faction.text: House Hornwood of Hornwood is a noble house of the North, seated at Hornwood and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms a bull moose, brown upon a dark orange field, and their words are 'Righteous in Wrath'. Their lands encompass the forest of the Hornwood and holdings near their seat, bordering those of House Bolton and House Manderly, and extending west to the White Knife. Their ruler is styled the Lord of the Hornwood and marriages have often bound the Hornwoods to houses such as Karstark, Flint, and Tallhart. During the Dance of the Dragons, Lord Hornwood declared for the blacks and marched south with Lord Cregan Stark, where his younger son was lost in King’s Landing during the Hour of the Wolf, and in the years that followed, men of the house were among those who formed The Wolf Pack is a sellsword company operating in Essos.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_17}House Ironsmith
Faction.text: House Ironsmith is a northern house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_18}House Mormont of Bear Island
Faction.text: House Mormont of Bear Island is a noble house of the North, seated upon Bear Island in the Bay of Ice and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms a black bear upon a green wood, and their words are 'Here We Stand'. The Mormonts are an ancient house of the First Men, and according to northern tradition, Bear Island was granted to them by a Stark king after being won from an ironborn ruler. Their lands are cold, remote, and poor in resources, and their hall is built of great logs and defended by an earthen palisade, reflecting the harsh conditions of the isle. The Mormonts possess the Valyrian steel sword Longclaw, held by their house for centuries, and both men and women of Bear Island are known to take up arms in its defense, owing to frequent threats from ironborn raiders and wildlings, giving the house a reputation for hardiness, honor, and resilience in the North.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_19}House Glover of Deepwood Motte
Faction.text: House Glover of Deepwood Motte is a noble house of the North, seated at Deepwood Motte and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a silver mailed fist upon a scarlet field. The Glovers trace their lineage to the First Men and once ruled as kings after the Long Night before being brought to vassalage by the Kings of Winter. Their lands lie within the Wolfswood along the western coast, where their seat guards the forested approaches. In later centuries, Benton Glover served as First Ranger of the Night’s Watch at the time of Queen Alysanne Targaryen’s visit in 58 AC, and the house aided Lord Cregan Stark in the hunting of the free folk raiders led by Sylas the Grim, marking them as a loyal and enduring house of the North.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_20}House Tallhart of Torrhen's Square
Faction.text: House Tallhart of Torrhen’s Square is a noble house of the North, seated at Torrhen’s Square southwest of Winterfell and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms three green sentinel trees upon a brown field, and their words are 'Proud and Free'. The Tallharts descend from the First Men and hold lands of forest and field about their seat, standing as a steadfast and dutiful house within the northern domain.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_21}House Dustin of Barrowton
Faction.text: House Dustin of Barrowton is a noble house of the North, seated at Barrow Hall in the town of Barrowton and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, counted among their principal bannermen. They claim descent from the First King of the First Men and the ancient Barrow Kings who ruled the barrowlands, a lineage accepted in the chronicles, and their rule over Barrowton has endured since the submission of the last Barrow King to the Kings of Winter. Barrowton prospered under their governance, and the house has long served the Starks with loyalty. Kings of the Targaryen line are recorded to have held court there, including Aegon I, and in 58 AC King Jaehaerys I and Queen Alysanne were received at Barrowton, where the Great Barrow was shown and a tourney held in their honor. House Dustin declared for Princess Rhaenyra during the Dance of the Dragons, and Lord Roderick Dustin led the host known as the Winter Wolves in the south, winning renown in battle before falling in the fighting, having slain notable foes before his death. In later years, Barrowton endured the Winter Fever, and during a harsh winter soon after, its lord closed the gates against starving smallfolk, a deed recorded among the harsher judgments of the North. Their arms portray two rusted longaxes with black shafts crossed, a black crown between their points, on yellow. The rusted crown on their arms derives from their claim of descent from the First King and the Barrow Kings that followed him.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_22}House Stout of Goldgrass
Faction.text: House Stout of Goldgrass is a noble house of the North, seated at Goldgrass and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms chevronny russet and gold. The Stouts are an ancient house of First Men descent, their lineage tracing back to the age before the coming of the Andals, and their name, short and unadorned, is held to reflect that ancient origin. Counted among the lesser houses of the North, they endure as part of the old blood that has long served beneath the Kings of Winter and their successors.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_23}House Glenmore of Rillwater Crossing
Faction.text: House Glenmore of Rillwater Crossing is a northern house seated at Rillwater Crossing in the Rills. They bear for arms a drawn bow and arrow containing a water wheel, all in white upon a dark red field, and their words are 'Never Relent, Never Yield'. Counted among the houses of the Rills, they hold their place within the northern domain, owing fealty to Winterfell and the ancient line of the Kings of Winter.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_24}House Ryswell of the Rills
Faction.text: House Ryswell of the Rills is a noble house of the North, seated in the Rills and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, counted among their prominent bannermen. They bear for arms a black horse’s head, with red eyes and mane, upon a bronze field within a black engrailed border. The Ryswells rule the wide lands between the barrowlands, the Stony Shore, and Blazewater Bay. In ancient days the Rills were held by the First Men kings of House Ryder, who submitted to the Kings of Winter, and though the manner by which House Ryswell came to rule these lands is not recorded, their dominion has long endured. In the histories it is told that King Theon Stark once quelled rebellion in the Rills, and an older tale recounts how a Lord Ryswell returned his own son, a deserter of the Night’s Watch, to the Wall to face judgment to be entombed alive in ice to forever stand the watch they had abandoned. Later Lord Ryswell took the black himself in his final years to watch over his son. Lady Robyn Ryswell is also recorded as having wed Jonnel Stark, Lord of Winterfell, further binding the house to the Stark line.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_25}House Marsh
Faction.text: House Marsh is a northern house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms ten green frogs arranged four, three, two, and one upon a yellow field. Despite their seat's proximity to the Neck, they consider themselves lowlanders.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_26}House Flint of Flint's Finger
Faction.text: House Flint of Flint’s Finger is a noble house of the North, seated at Flint’s Finger and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a grey stone hand upon a white inverted pall on a field paly black and grey, oft referred to as the stone hand of House Flint. They are one of several branches of the Flint name, distinct from those of Breakstone Hill and Widow’s Watch. In the annals of the Night’s Watch, Lord Commander Rodrik Flint is remembered for his failed attempt to crown himself King-Beyond-the-Wall, and among the darker tales of the Nightfort is the murder of a young woman named Danny Flint. It is also counted among the many suggestions that the Night’s King may have been of Flint blood. In the years following the Dance of the Dragons, Timotty Snow, a bastard of Flint’s Finger, is recorded to have helped found the company known as the Wolf Pack alongside Hallis Hornwood.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_27}House Reed of Greywater Watch
Faction.text: House Reed of Greywater Watch is a noble house of the North, seated at Greywater Watch within the Neck and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a black lizard-lion upon a grey-green field. The Reeds have long been counted among the most steadfast of Stark bannermen, first swearing fealty when the Starks ruled as Kings in the North. Since the defeat of the Marsh Kings, House Reed has led the crannogmen of the Neck, maintaining dominion over its swamps and waterways and guarding the southern approaches to the North with vigilance and cunning born of their land.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_28}House Fenn
Faction.text: House Fenn is a northern house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms three black water lilies upon a pale violet field.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_29}House Blackmyre
Faction.text: House Blackmyre is a house of the crannogmen of the Neck, sworn to House Reed of Greywater Watch and through them to House Stark of Winterfell. They dwell within the bogs and waterways of the Neck, living according to the customs of the crannogmen and the traditions of the First Men.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_30}House Cray
Faction.text: House Cray is a house of the crannogmen of the Neck, sworn to House Reed of Greywater Watch and through them to House Stark of Winterfell. They dwell within the shifting bogs and waterways of the Neck, where land and water are ever in flux.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_31}House Peat
Faction.text: House Peat is a house of the crannogmen of the Neck, sworn to House Reed of Greywater Watch and through them to House Stark of Winterfell. They dwell within the marshes and bogs of the Neck, living according to the customs of the crannogmen and the traditions of the First Men.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_32}House Poole
Faction.text: House Poole of the North is a noble house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a blue plate upon a white field. Counted among the lesser houses of the North, they hold their place within the domain of Winterfell, owing fealty to the ancient line of the Kings of Winter and their successors.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_33}House Cassel
Faction.text: House Cassel of the North is a noble house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms six white wolves upon a grey field bordered black. Counted among the lesser houses, the Cassels are long associated with service to Winterfell, holding their place within the northern domain through loyalty and duty to the Stark line.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_34}House Manderly of White Harbor
Faction.text: House Manderly of White Harbor is a noble house of the North, seated at the New Castle within the city of White Harbor and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms a white merman with dark green hair, beard, and tail, bearing a black trident upon a blue-green field. Though now of the North, the Manderlys are an ancient line who once dwelt along the river Mander in the Reach, where they held Dunstonbury and contended with House Peake before being driven into exile by the Gardeners, thereafter finding refuge with the Starks, who granted them the Wolf’s Den and charged them with the defense of the White Knife. Bringing their wealth with them, they raised White Harbor into a prosperous city and chief port of the North, later constructing the New Castle in likeness of their former seat. Unlike most northern houses, they keep the Faith of the Seven and uphold strong traditions of knighthood. In the centuries since their arrival, they have remained steadfast in their loyalty to Winterfell, with their dominion extending across the lands about the White Knife and supported by numerous lesser lords and landed knights sworn to their service.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_35}House Locke of Oldcastle
Faction.text: House Locke of Oldcastle is a noble house of the North, seated at Oldcastle and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a bronze key upon a purple field. Counted among the houses of the eastern North, their lands lie along the coast near the Bite, where they serve within the northern domain under the authority of Winterfell and maintain their place among its sworn bannermen.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_36}House Woolfield
Faction.text: House Woolfield is a noble house of the North, sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They blazon their arms with three woolsacks, white on purple, a white border.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_37}House Flint of Widow's Watch
Faction.text: House Flint of Widow’s Watch is a noble house of the North, seated at Widow’s Watch and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a blue field strewn with whitecaps, upon a yellow chief with a crested line displaying a pair of blue eyes, and their words are 'Ever Vigilant'. They are one of several branches of House Flint, distinct from those of Breakstone Hill and Flint’s Finger. In the annals of the Night’s Watch, Lord Commander Rodrik Flint is remembered for his attempt to crown himself King-Beyond-the-Wall, and among the darker tales of the Nightfort is the murder of a young woman bearing the Flint name. It is also counted among the many suggestions that the Night’s King may have been of Flint blood.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_38}House Slate of Blackpool
Faction.text: House Slate of Blackpool is a noble house of the North, seated at Blackpool and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms pale grey with a double tressure of white. The Slates trace their lineage to the First Men and are said to have ruled as kings after the Long Night before submitting to the Kings of Winter and becoming vassals of House Stark. In earlier ages, House Slate is recorded to have held the Wolf’s Den, marking them among the older powers of the eastern North whose authority was later diminished under Stark dominion.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_39}House Lightfoot
Faction.text: House Lightfoot is a northern house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_40}House Forrester of Ironrath
Faction.text: House Forrester of Ironrath is a noble house of the North, seated at Ironrath and sworn to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a white ironwood tree upon a black field.
