The Forresters are of First Men descent and hold dominion over the great ironwood forests of the wolfswood, from which they derive their strength and standing, for ironwood is a resource of great value in the North. Their seat at Ironrath lies deep within these woods, and their rule is closely tied to the harvesting and defense of their timber. In the chronicles, they are known to be in rivalry with House Whitehill, a conflict rooted in control of ironwood and the lands of the northern hills, marking them as a house whose fortunes are bound to both forest and feud within the northern domain.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_41}House Branch
Faction.text: House Branch is a northern house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_42}House Woods
Faction.text: House Woods is a northern house sworn to House Stark of Winterfell.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_43}House Magnar of Kingshouse
Faction.text: House Magnar of Kingshouse is a ruling house of Skagos, seated at Kingshouse and long counted among those subject to House Stark of Winterfell, though their loyalty has ever been uncertain. They bear for arms a green lobster upon a white field, its claws gripping a black harpoon. In recent years, the Skagosi, grown restless under the customs and authority of the mainland, have raised the Magnar as High King, casting aside their fealty and uniting beneath his rule. From Kingshouse, the Magnar commands the clans of Skagos, drawing strength from the island’s harsh mountains and storm-lashed coasts, where the old ways endure and defiance of Winterfell has taken root once more.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_44}House Stane of Driftwood Hall
Faction.text: House Stane of Driftwood Hall is a noble house of Skagos, seated at Driftwood Hall and counted among the clans sworn to the Magnar of Kingshouse, though in earlier times they were subjects of House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms a black longship upon a grey field. The Stanes are of First Men descent and dwell along the harsh coasts of Skagos, where the sea shapes their way of life and strength, and like other Skagosi, they are known for their fierce independence and adherence to the old ways, standing now among those who follow the Magnar in defiance of northern rule.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NORTH_45}House Crowl of Deepdown
Faction.text: House Crowl of Deepdown is a noble house of Skagos, seated at Deepdown and counted among the clans sworn to the Magnar of Kingshouse, though in earlier times they were subject to House Stark of Winterfell. They bear for arms three black crows upon a white field. The Crowls dwell in the rugged interior of Skagos, where caves, cliffs, and cold stone define their lands, and they are known as a fierce and insular people, keeping to the old ways and standing among those who follow the Magnar in defiance of northern rule.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_1}House Greyjoy of Pyke
Faction.text: House Greyjoy of Pyke is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at Pyke and ruling as Lords of the Iron Islands, sworn to the Iron Throne. They bear for arms a golden kraken upon a black field, and their words are 'We Do Not Sow'. The Greyjoys are of the ironborn, a people shaped by the harsh seas and the old way, whose customs prize reaving and strength above tilling the land. In earlier ages, the Iron Islands were ruled by kings of the isles and the rivers, until their power was broken and their dominion reduced, after which House Greyjoy rose as the principal house of the isles. From Pyke, they command the captains and lords of the islands, maintaining their authority over a realm ever bound to the sea and the enduring traditions of the ironborn. Recently, Dagon Greyjoy the Lord Reaper of Pyke has grown restless and begun reaving the Sunset Sea.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_2}House Botley of Lordsport
Faction.text: House Botley of Lordsport is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at Lordsport upon the island of Pyke and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms a shoal of silver fish upon a pale green field. Their seat lies across the island from the Greyjoy stronghold, serving as a center of harbor and trade, and their hall is a great structure of timber and wattle. In the years following the death of Dalton Greyjoy, the house suffered greatly during the westermen’s invasion under Lady Johanna Lannister, when the Lord Botley of that time was slain, marking a grim chapter in their history amid the strife of the Iron Islands.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_3}House Wynch of Iron Holt
Faction.text: House Wynch of Iron Holt is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at Iron Holt upon the northern shore of Pyke and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms a bloody moon upon a purple field. Their seat lies across the island from the Greyjoy stronghold, guarding the northern coast. In the turmoil that followed the death of Dalton Greyjoy, Alester Wynch joined with Gunthor Goodbrother in holding Faircastle, though their alliance ended in blood when Gunthor slew Alester in a quarrel over Lysa Farman, a deed remembered among the strife of that troubled time.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_4}House Saltcliffe
Faction.text: House Saltcliffe of Saltcliffe is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon the isle of Saltcliffe and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms a nine-headed serpent, black upon a silver field. The Saltcliffes are among the ironborn, a people shaped by the sea and the old way, and their isle lies among the lesser islands of the archipelago, where they maintain their strength through seamanship and reaving, holding their place among the captains and lords who follow the Greyjoys.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_5}House Sunderly of Saltcliffe
Faction.text: House Sunderly of Saltcliffe is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon the isle of Saltcliffe and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms a drowned man, pink and pale, floating upright in a blue-green sea, his hair streaming upwards, as fish nibble at his limbs. The Sunderlys are counted among the ironborn, and like the other houses of the isles, they are shaped by the customs of the Drowned God and the harsh life of the sea, holding their place among the captains and lords who follow the Greyjoys.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_6}House Farwynd of Lonely Light
Faction.text: House Farwynd of the Lonely Light is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at the Lonely Light, the chief isle of a remote cluster lying eight days’ sail northwest of Great Wyk, and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms per fesse, beneath a black sea with crested line a black longship set against a setting sun, dark red upon orange. Their origins are traced to a captain blown off course in ancient days, who raised a beacon upon the isle and took the name Farwynd, his descendants continuing to rule there ever since. The Farwynds of the Lonely Light are held apart from other ironborn, regarded as strange, with tales told of their dealings with beasts of the sea and of skinchanging, though such accounts are spoken of with uncertainty. Despite their isolation, they remain within the order of the isles, and a Lord Farwynd is recorded among those present at the Great Council of 101 AC, marking their place among the lords of the realm.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_7}House Farwynd of Sealskin Point
Faction.text: House Farwynd of the Sealskin Point is a cadet branch of House Farwynd of the Iron Islands, seated at the Sealskin Point, located on the northern coast of Great Wyk, and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms per fesse, beneath a black sea with crested line a black longship set against a setting sun, yellow upon blue. Their origins are traced to a captain blown off course in ancient days, who raised a beacon upon the isle and took the name Farwynd. The Farwynds of Sealskin are held apart from other ironborn, regarded as strange, with tales told of their dealings with beasts of the sea and of skinchanging, though such accounts are spoken of with uncertainty.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_8}House Goodbrother of Hammerhorn
Faction.text: House Goodbrother of Hammerhorn is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at Hammerhorn upon Great Wyk and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms a gold-banded black warhorn upon a red field. Their seat lies inland in the Hardstone Hills, and unlike many ironborn houses, their wealth is drawn not from the Sunset Sea but from mines beneath their lands. House Goodbrother is divided into several branches upon Great Wyk, including those of Corpse Lake, Crow Spike Keep, and Downdelving, with further branches on Old Wyk and Orkmont. They claim descent not from the Grey King himself, but from his elder brother, and in the days when kingsmoots chose the High King, the Goodbrothers were second only to House Greyiron in the number of kings raised from their line, among them Urrathon IV, called 'Badbrother'. In later years, Gunthor Goodbrother slew Alester Wynch during the struggles following the death of Dalton Greyjoy, and in the war that followed the westermen’s invasion of the isles in 134 AC, Arthur Goodbrother slew Ser Leo Costayne before the lord of the house was himself slain, marking a turbulent chapter in their history.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_9}House Humble
Faction.text: House Humble is a house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms chains and a broken shackle upon a black field. The Humbles are regarded as a lesser house, said to descend from thralls and slaves, a lineage reflected in their name and arms. Despite this lowly origin, they have risen to hold a place among the ironborn, bound to the customs of the isles and the rule of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_10}House Merlyn of Pebbleton
Faction.text: House Merlyn of Pebbleton is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at Pebbleton and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms twining waterspouts, green upon a white field. Counted among the ironborn houses, the Merlyns hold their place along the stony coasts of the isles, their strength drawn from the sea and the traditions of the Drowned God, serving among the captains and lords who uphold the rule of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_11}House Sparr
Faction.text: House Sparr is a noble house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke, with their lands lying some six leagues from Hammerhorn upon Great Wyk. Their lord is styled 'the Sparr', in keeping with the customs of the ironborn. They bear for arms an oak saltire upon a blue field. Counted among the houses of Great Wyk, they hold their place within the order of the isles, bound to the traditions of the Drowned God and the rule of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_12}House Drumm of Old Wyk
Faction.text: House Drumm of Old Wyk is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon Old Wyk and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms a white bone hand upon a red field. The Drumms are an ancient line, with their history stretching back many centuries upon Old Wyk, the holiest of the ironborn isles and among the first settled by the First Men. In ancient days, members of the house ruled as rock kings, and Regnar Drumm, called Raven-feeder, was chosen High King by kingsmoot after the abdication of a Greyiron king. The Drumms later took part in the downfall of House Greyiron alongside other great houses of the isles. Among their number are remembered figures such as Dale the Dread and Roryn the Reaver, while tales are told of Dagon Drumm, said to have practiced dark arts, and Gormond the Oldfather, who is claimed to have sired a great many sons. Hilmar the Cunning is said to have won the Valyrian steel sword Red Rain through guile rather than strength. In more recent history, the Lord Drumm of Old Wyk was slain during the westermen’s invasion of the Iron Islands under Lady Johanna Lannister in 134 AC, marking a grievous loss during that conflict.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_13}House Stonehouse of Old Wyk
Faction.text: House Stonehouse of Old Wyk is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon Old Wyk and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke, counted among their principal bannermen. They bear for arms a black brazier upon a grey masonry field. The Stonehouses are among the chief houses of Old Wyk, holding their place within the order of the isles through strength and adherence to the customs of the ironborn. In the turmoil that followed the death of Dalton Greyjoy, the lord of the house was slain during the westermen’s invasion under Lady Johanna Lannister in 134 AC, marking a grievous loss in a time of strife for the islands.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_14}House Orkwood of Orkmont
Faction.text: House Orkwood of Orkmont is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon the island of Orkmont and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms dark green pines strewn closely together upon a yellow field. The Orkwoods rule upon one of the largest and richest of the isles, a rugged and mountainous land abundant in ore such as iron, lead, and tin, though its forests have long been diminished through shipbuilding. Orkmont holds several notable seats, including those of House Orkwood and House Tawney, as well as a branch of House Goodbrother. In ancient days, the island was home to the iron kings of Houses Greyiron and Hoare, and in the struggles that ended the rule of the Greyirons, the Orkwoods were among those who joined the alliance of ironborn lords and Andals that brought down King Rognar II Greyiron, marking their place in the long history of the isles.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_15}House Tawney of Orkmont
Faction.text: House Tawney is a noble house of the Iron Island sworn directly to House Greyjoy and is one of their strongest bannermen. Their lands are on the island of Orkmont. Their sigil is a scourge of nettles, red and black, on white.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_16}House Blacktyde
Faction.text: House Blacktyde of Blacktyde is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon the isle of Blacktyde and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke, counted among their foremost bannermen. They bear for arms vairy green and black. In the chronicles of the isles, it is said that Qhored I Hoare may have been of their blood, and in ancient days High King Joron I Blacktyde is remembered for capturing Gyles II Gardener at sea, putting him to death, and carrying war into the Reach. In later history, following the death of Dalton Greyjoy at Faircastle, the Blacktydes joined with House Harlaw and others in raising Rodrik Greyjoy as successor, a struggle that plunged the Iron Islands into months of violent contention, marking them as a house long entangled in the power and tumult of the isles.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_17}House Codd
Faction.text: House Codd is a noble house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms a silver codfish upon a black field, and their words are 'Though All Men Do Despise Us'. The Codds are held in low regard among the ironborn, with their name spoken with scorn in the isles, yet they endure as part of the ironborn order, bound to the customs of the Drowned God and the rule of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_18}House Sharp
Faction.text: House Sharp is a noble house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_19}House Weaver
Faction.text: House Weaver is a house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms two white sewing needles upon a brown field. Counted among the younger and lesser houses of the isles, the Weavers hold low standing among the ironborn, yet remain part of the order of captains and lords bound to the customs of the Drowned God and the rule of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_20}House Netley
Faction.text: House Netley is a noble house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_21}House Harlaw of Ten Towers
Faction.text: House Harlaw of Harlaw is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at Ten Towers upon the isle of Harlaw and sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke, counted among the most powerful houses of the isles. They bear for arms a silver scythe upon a black field. From Ten Towers they rule the whole of Harlaw, the richest and most populous of the Iron Islands, with the lesser houses of the isle, including Kenning, Myre, Stonetree, and Volmark, sworn to their authority. In ancient days the Harlaws ruled as rock and salt kings, and several of their line were chosen as High Kings through the kingsmoot. Among them, Erich V Harlaw won Fair Isle for the ironborn, while his son Harron slew Gareth II Gardener beneath the walls of Oldtown. Though the ending of the kingsmoot reduced them from kings to lords, the Harlaws have remained among the foremost powers of the isles. In the present age, Lord Theomore Harlaw has raised the great castle of Ten Towers in place of Harlaw Hall, further increasing the strength and stature of his house.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_22}House Harlaw of Harlaw Hall
Faction.text: House Harlaw of Harlaw Hall is a cadet branch of House Harlaw upon the isle of Harlaw, seated at Harlaw Hall and sworn to the lord of Ten Towers. They bear for arms per bend black and silver, two scythes countercharged. Harlaw Hall was once the principal seat of the Harlaws until the reign of Lord Theomore Harlaw, who, having lost three infant sons to the damp and unhealthy conditions of the old castle, resolved to raise a new seat upon better ground. Being a man of changing mind, Theomore built ten separate keeps, later joined by wallwalks and covered bridges, and from this work arose the castle known as Ten Towers, thereafter leaving Harlaw Hall to a cadet branch of his line.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_23}House Volmark
Faction.text: House Volmark of Volmark is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated at Volmark upon the isle of Harlaw and sworn to House Harlaw of Ten Towers. They bear for arms a black leviathan upon a grey sea. The Volmarks hold broad lands upon Harlaw and are counted among its foremost houses. In the years following the fall of Harren Hoare at Harrenhal, Lord Qhorin Volmark laid claim to the Iron Islands through his descent from the line of Harwyn Hardhand and declared himself king, but he was slain by King Aegon I Targaryen during the conquest of the isles. A later conspiracy to raise his son likewise failed under the rule of Goren Greyjoy. In more recent memory, the Lord Volmark of the day was slain during the westermen’s invasion of the Iron Islands under Lady Johanna Lannister in 134 AC, adding another grievous loss to the history of the house.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_24}House Kenning of Harlaw
Faction.text: House Kenning of Harlaw is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon the isle of Harlaw and sworn to House Harlaw. They bear for arms the cloudy hand of the Storm God, pale grey with yellow lightning flashing from its fingertips, upon a black field. In ancient days, the Kennings were bitter foes of House Harlaw, but they were defeated and brought beneath the rule of the Harlaws. The line is remembered also for Herrock Kenning, an ironborn warrior of Harlaw, who crossed to the western shore of the green lands and founded the branch of House Kenning at Kayce.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_25}House Myre
Faction.text: House Myre of Harlaw is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon the isle of Harlaw and sworn to House Harlaw. They bear for arms ten black nooses, four, three, two, and one, upon a white field with a border of blood. In ancient days the Myres were bitter foes of House Harlaw, but they were defeated and brought beneath Harlaw rule, thereafter holding their place among the lesser lords of the isle.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_26}House Stonetree
Faction.text: House Stonetree of Harlaw is a noble house of the Iron Islands, seated upon the isle of Harlaw and sworn to House Harlaw. They bear for arms a bare stone tree, grey upon a black field. The Stonetrees hold broad lands upon Harlaw and are counted among its more notable houses, their line possessing a long and distinguished history within the order of the isles, though they remain bound in fealty to the Harlaws.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_27}House Ironmaker
Faction.text: House Ironmaker is a noble house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. They bear for arms a grey anvil and hammer upon a black field. Counted among the ironborn houses, the Ironmakers hold their place within the isles through strength, craft, and adherence to the customs of the Drowned God and the rule of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_IRON_28}House Shepherd
Faction.text: House Shepherd is a noble house of the Iron Islands, sworn to House Greyjoy of Pyke. Counted among the younger and lesser houses of the isles, they possess little stature or influence beside the greater captains and lords, yet remain bound to the customs of the Drowned God and the rule of Pyke.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NW_1}Castle Black
Faction.text: Castle Black is the chief seat of the Night’s Watch and the residence of its Lord Commander, standing at the center of the Wall upon the kingsroad. In the year 196 AC it is commanded by Jack Musgood, known among his brothers as Jolly Jack. From Castle Black the Watch governs its remaining strength, dispatching rangers beyond the Wall, overseeing the order’s stores and recruits, and maintaining watch upon the central stretch of the Wall. Though the Night’s Watch is diminished from the power it held in earlier ages, Castle Black remains the heart of the order, with its great gate beneath the Wall, its training yard, armory, and timber halls serving as the center of the black brothers' rule and labor.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NW_2}Westwatch
Faction.text: Westwatch-by-the-Bridge is one of the major castles of the Night’s Watch, situated at the western end of the Wall near the Bridge of Skulls guarding the western approaches beyond the Wall. Westwatch-by-the-Bridge serves as an important post for ranging and defense, standing watch over the bridge and the lands where the foothills of the Frostfangs draw near to the Wall.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NW_3}The Shadow Tower
Faction.text: The Shadow Tower stands at the western end of the Wall beside the northern mountains, lying west of Sentinel Stand and east of Westwatch-by-the-Bridge. Counted among the three castles still inhabited by the Night’s Watch. It guards the western approaches where raiders and free folk most often descend from the Frostfangs. The Gorge beyond the Wall is known as a perilous route by which free folk sometimes pass around the tower. In earlier centuries, the ranger Redwyn wrote of a ranging from the Shadow Tower to Lorn Point upon the Frozen Shore, during which his party fought giants and treated with the children of the forest. The castle is also remembered in darker tales of the Watch, among them the story of a criminal cook sent to the Wall, who bore seven-pointed stars burned into his flesh as self-inflicted mark for how many Septas he had raped. His left arm had stars from wrist to elbow, and stars marked his calves as well.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NW_4}Stonedoor
Faction.text: Stonedoor is a castle of the Night’s Watch upon the Wall, lying between Greyguard to the west and Hoarfrost Hill to the east. The lands about it are marked by low hills, making the ground near the Wall more uneven than elsewhere along its length. Stonedoor still stands as one of the strongholds raised in earlier ages, when the Night’s Watch possessed the strength to man far more castles than in the present day.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NW_5}Deep Lake
Faction.text: Deep Lake is a castle of the Night’s Watch upon the Wall, lying east of the Nightfort and west of Queensgate, where the Wall bends about the southern shore of a deep green lake. A stream from the lake flows westward to the Milkwater. The castle was raised during the reign of King Jaehaerys I Targaryen as a smaller and less costly replacement for the Nightfort, after Queen Alysanne persuaded Lord Commander Lothor Burley that the older fortress should be abandoned. Paid for with jewels given by the queen and built over eight years by workmen sent north by the king, Deep Lake stands with a statue of Queen Alysanne before its hall.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NW_6}Oakenshield
Faction.text: Oakenshield is a castle of the Night's Watch upon the Wall, lying east of Castle Black and west of Woodswatch-by-the-Pool.

Faction.name: {=LOC_NW_7}Eastwatch
Faction.text: Eastwatch-by-the-Sea is the easternmost castle of the Night's Watch, standing upon the grey and windswept shore of the Bay of Seals, with the abandoned castle of Greenguard lying to its west. Eastwatch serves as the order's chief harbor and naval station. The Night's Watch maintains galleys there, from lean fighting craft to larger vessels capable of crossing the narrow sea, using them to patrol the Bay of Seals, pursue smugglers, and watch the eastern waters. Fisherfolk dwell near the castle, and merchants from across the narrow sea sometimes call at its harbor, making Eastwatch a place where free folk occasionally come to trade with the Watch. The brothers of Eastwatch also breed sure-footed mules suited to the icy heights of the Wall. In earlier years, Maester Balder wrote his work 'The Edge of the World' whilst serving there, and it is recorded that Corlys Velaryon visited Eastwatch aboard the Ice Wolf.
